target.title: feat(msteams): Microsoft Teams voice (CVI) + video + chat + governance
target.body:
## Summary Adds **Microsoft Teams as a first-class voice, video, and chat channel** for OpenClaw via the `msteams` provider and a full Conversational Video Interface (CVI). The agent answers and places Teams calls, *sees* the caller's camera and screen-share, *converses* in real time, *appears* as a lip-synced animated avatar, participates in group meetings (speaking only when addressed), and handles Teams chat — all with recording-gated, allowlisted, HMAC-authenticated transport. Teams audio/video cannot reach OpenClaw's existing webhook/media plane: Teams calls are delivered through Graph Calling signalling + the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` socket, which only run on .NET/Windows. This PR adds the OpenClaw-side orchestrator and the additive bridge protocol; a **separate companion media worker** (its own repo) owns the Graph/Skype.Bots.Media socket and renders the avatar tile. The three CVI pillars map directly onto what lands here: | Pillar | Meaning | In this PR | | --- | --- | --- | | **Perception** | the agent *sees* | inbound `video.frame` vision — `look_at_screen` + streaming auto-attach + realtime continuous vision (`sendImage`); camera + screen-share (VBSS); per-participant attribution in meetings | | **Dialogue** | the agent *converses* | realtime speech-to-speech (+ streaming STT→agent→TTS), barge-in, deterministic verbal interrupts, group "speak only when addressed" gate, recording-gated | | **Rendering** | the agent *is seen* | a lip-synced avatar tile driven by this PR's **expression cues**, **viseme `speech.marks`**, and **`show_to_caller`/`display.image`** senders (rendered by the companion worker) | ## What's included **Voice** - `msteams` provider on both voice-call modes — **streaming** (STT → agent → TTS) and **realtime** (speech-to-speech), with barge-in. - Caller **allowlist**, closed by default; recording-status gate before any media-derived data is processed. - **Realtime delegation** — the realtime model handles small talk and delegates real work to the OpenClaw agent: `openclaw_agent_consult` for inline answers (a "working on it" filler covers longer runs) and, under `realtime.toolPolicy: "owner"`, `openclaw_agent_task` for background jobs. - **Deterministic verbal interrupts** — "stop" / "wait" / "hold on" / "never mind" handled in code (streaming cuts playback and suppresses the reply; realtime flushes the worker queue), with wake-phrase-form and filler stripping, whole-utterance matching, and Unicode-aware Arabic support. - **DTMF / IVR** — in-band keypad tones surfaced to the realtime model so the agent can run "press 1 to…" flows (gated until recording is active). - **Bilingual Arabic/English** (opt-in) — detect, mirror, switch-with-caller, translate on request; the deterministic layers (interrupts, visemes) understand Arabic regardless. - **Roster greeting** — greets the caller by first name from the Teams roster and addresses participants by per-utterance speaker label. **Inbound video vision** - `video.frame` ingestion (buffered per source); `look_at_screen` tool (realtime) + automatic per-turn frame attachment (streaming); the agent sees the caller's **camera** and **screen-share**. - **Realtime continuous vision** — `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage` (a small core plugin-sdk addition + openai/azure realtime provider) pushes the latest changed frame as an ambient `input_image` item every ~6 s, no forced response, budget-capped and recording-gated. - **Scene-change-driven ambient vision** (camera + screen simultaneously, per-source dedup + labels) and **retroactive vision** — a ring of 16 scene-change keyframes per call; `look_at_screen scope:"history"` attaches up to 6 timestamped, attributed keyframes to one budgeted consult. - Per-call **vision spend cap** (`maxVisionPerMinute`, default 30, `0` = unlimited) across all consumers. **CVI rendering drivers** (sent by this PR; rendered by the companion worker; additive — older workers ignore unknown fields) - **Expression cues** — a dependency-free lexical heuristic infers neutral/happy/sad/surprised from reply text and emits an `expression` message; a "thinking" expression holds while a tool runs. - **Viseme lip-sync** — `speech.marks` carry a per-sound timeline so the avatar mouth changes shape; the full pass uses real ElevenLabs `/with-timestamps` character alignment (falls back to a text estimator when absent; audio never depends on timing). - **Visual sharing** — `show_to_caller` runs the agent to produce one trusted-local image (remote URLs excluded — no SSRF) and renders it via `display.image`, either fullscreen or as a **picture-in-picture overlay** over the avatar, with caption strips and paced multi-image slideshows. **Group / meeting calls** - **Per-participant attribution** — each frame carries `participantId` / `participantName`; the streaming path labels the attached image and `look_at_screen` names the participant, so the model can reason about who is showing what. Active-speaker camera follow / re-select. - **Speak only when addressed** — in a group call (2+ humans) the assistant stays silent until addressed by name (`wakePhrases`), with a short follow-up window; 1:1 always responds. Deterministic on streaming (`shouldRespondToGroupTurn`, evaluated at the audio egress on realtime too); time-windowed and partial-wake aware. **Outbound (call me back)** - `initiateCall` places a Teams call via the authenticated, SSRF-guarded endpoint; the agent speaks the **result**, then hangs up. Delivery via Teams **chat message** (default) or **voice call-back**. - **Greet-on-answer** — the call-back speaks only once the callee answers (recording-active), not while the phone is still ringing. - Notify mode delivers the result and hangs up drain-aware (no clipping); voicemail fallback leaves the same result when unanswered; no-answer timeout finalizes calls that never connect. **Chat & governance** - **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** — a Teams message-context command quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path (requires the app manifest to declare the `askOpenClaw` command). - **Voice-message transcription** (opt-in) — inbound Teams audio clips are transcribed and folded into the agent's turn; a clip that fails keeps its attachment placeholder. - **Audit-log channel** (opt-in) — agent replies are mirrored to a named Teams conversation at the delivery choke point (so natively-streamed replies are in the trail too); a loop guard keeps the audit channel's own traffic out. - **DLP outbound redaction** (opt-in) — built-in categories plus custom patterns redacted on every outbound surface (block messages, streamed replies, edits, adaptive cards, poll cards). Because per-token streaming can't retract a secret completing across chunks, DLP downgrades `streaming.mode: "partial"` to `"progress"`. **Meeting productivity** - **End-of-meeting recap** (opt-in) — key points / decisions / action items posted to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or into the meeting thread (group) at call end. - **On-demand minutes** mid-call (`post_meeting_minutes`), a real **OOXML `.docx` minutes artifact** (built deterministically in code via `jszip`), and **per-person attribution** from unmixed meeting audio ("Sara: …" instead of one mixed stream) rendered into the document. **Sessions & memory** - Caller identity (Teams AAD object id, else a per-call-unique id) keys the session; `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys by Teams meeting/chat thread so parallel conversations stay isolated. PSTN falls through to per-phone. ## Architecture OpenClaw runs the orchestration in this PR — the `msteams` voice-call provider, the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket bridge, STT/agent/TTS and realtime delegation, the group/vision/governance logic, and all CVI **drivers** (`expression`, `speech.marks`, `display.image`, `sendImage`). A **separate companion Windows/.NET media worker** (its own repo) owns the Graph Calling signalling and the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` socket, and renders the avatar tile (RMS lip-sync blended with viseme shapes, head bob/sway, blink, gaze, expression-shaped mouths, and the `display.image` overlay). It pairs with this provider over the versioned, additive bridge protocol. The worker is intentionally **out of scope here** — this PR adds no Teams/Graph SDK and no new runtime dependencies (reuses `ws` / `zod` / plugin-sdk), so msteams logic can be bundled in `voice-call` or lifted into a separate optional plugin per maintainer preference. ## Recent reliability fixes - **Echo self-answer fix** — `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` now defaults **on** (default-off was a live self-answer bug); the echo guard runs on a playout clock, and the opening greeting allows no barge-in until the caller's first real turn, so a speakerphone echo can't make the bot re-greet in a loop. - **Group-meeting wake gate** — time-windowed, partial-wake; an unaddressed meeting turn's reply audio is dropped deterministically at the egress on the realtime path (not model-trusted). - **Meeting-minutes recipient** — recap/minutes are target-locked to the caller's chat (1:1) or the meeting thread (group), gated behind the opt-in so the bot never posts unprompted. - **Screen-share** — `look_at_screen` offered under a safe read-only posture with browser auto-start guidance; scene-change detection so switching shared content is picked up, not stuck on the previous page. - **Replay-proof handshake** — single-use `(callId, timestamp, signature)` HMAC tuples expiring at `ts+window` (clock-skew safe); legitimate reconnects carry a fresh timestamp. - **Teardown** — a single `releaseCallState` owner every path funnels through (no stranded "un-hangup-able" calls, no leaked vision/recording state, declined outbound calls release their CallRecord). ## Behavior changes Defaults preserve prior behavior except two flagged changes: - `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` flipped **off → on** (fixes the self-answer echo bug; set `false` to restore, tune via `echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `echoBargeInRms`). - Outbound call-backs honor `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify**: state the result, then hang up after audio drains) and **speak only on answer** (set `"conversation"` to keep the lingering behavior). All other additions are opt-in: `msteams.meetingRecap`, `msteams.bilingual`, `channels.msteams.transcribeVoiceMessages`, `channels.msteams.auditChannel`, `channels.msteams.dlp.enabled`. ## Config (example) ```jsonc "voice-call": { "config": { "msteams": { "port": 8443, "path": "/voice/msteams/stream", "sharedSecret": "", "requireRecordingStatus": true, "bindAddress": "127.0.0.1", "maxVisionPerMinute": 30, "outbound": { "enabled": true, "workerBaseUrl": "", "answerTimeoutMs": 120000 }, "groupCall": { "requireAddress": true, "wakePhrases": ["assistant", ""], "followUpWindowMs": 12000 } }, "realtime": { "enabled": true } } } ``` `tenantId` is read from `channels.msteams.tenantId`. `groupCall` only affects calls with 2+ humans; 1:1 always responds. `maxVisionPerMinute` caps per-call vision spend across `look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, and the realtime ambient push. Audio is **PCM 16 kHz**. ### Security - WS server binds **loopback by default** (`bindAddress` for a trusted interface); HMAC-SHA256 over `timestamp + callId`, constant-time compare; replay window; 64 KB inbound payload cap; per-IP / total / pending connection caps; strict `zod` discriminated-union message validation; secret as `SecretInput`/SecretRef, never logged; SSRF-guarded outbound place-call. Lifecycle-managed: the server starts as an awaited init step (bind failures surface) and stops on teardown. ## Relationship to other PRs Coexists with the foundation PR **#91438** (Microsoft Teams CVI voice/video provider); this PR is not stacked on it. ## Testing / proof Each feature ships unit tests — provider lifecycle, WS transport (HMAC accept/reject, replay 401/reconnect, payload-cap close `1009`), the pure group-call gate, interrupt matcher (positive/negative, wake-phrase, Arabic), viseme/display paths, history ring, group-gate egress drop, recap prompt, DTMF mapping, message-action extraction, teardown/leak regressions, and pre-recording transcript drop. Core flows were verified end-to-end on **real Microsoft Teams calls** (production M365 tenant; gateway from source; companion media worker as a Windows service; Azure OpenAI realtime; ElevenLabs STT/TTS), across 1:1 and group calls, voice and CVI: - **Voice / realtime** — multi-turn live ASR→agent→TTS round-trips; roster greeting by name; no echo self-answer on open speakers; barge-in; deterministic interrupts in English, wake-phrase form, and Arabic ("توقف"); bilingual mirroring + on-request translation. - **Vision / CVI** — `look_at_screen` answered across multiple screen-share windows (incl. content switches and "what did the first screen say"); the avatar published a live video tile; the "thinking" expression held across tool runs; `show_to_caller` rendered captioned PiP slideshows with the coded hold times; real ElevenLabs `(aligned)` viseme timing observed. - **Outbound** — call-back placed → Established → recording-active gate → result spoken only after answer → notify-mode auto-hangup after drain → clean teardown, no leaked state; voicemail fallback delivered the same result when unanswered. - **Meeting / chat / governance** — on-hangup recap + minutes landed in the Teams chat; background task delivered its answer to chat; audit mirror produced one line per streamed DM reply with no self-mirror inside the audit channel; a DLP-redacted email rendered as `[REDACTED:email]` on the streamed wire path; the "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action dispatched and replied. Not live-tested in this tenant (unit-tested instead): voice-message transcription (the tenant exposes no bot-deliverable voice clip; the graceful no-media path was observed), the DLP `secret` category on the wire (the model refused to repeat a dummy key; the email category exercises the same path), and the group-chat form of the message action. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Microsoft Teams voice/video/CVI + chat as a first-class OpenClaw channel — the `msteams` voice provider answers/places real Teams calls, sees the caller's screen-share/camera, renders a lip-synced avatar, and runs chat governance (DLP / audit / minutes). - **Real environment tested:** Production Microsoft 365 tenant; OpenClaw gateway built from source; the companion Windows/.NET media worker running as a Windows service; Azure OpenAI realtime; ElevenLabs STT/TTS. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Enabled `voice-call` `provider: "msteams"` + `realtime` and the `channels.msteams` governance blocks in `openclaw.json`, started the gateway (`openclaw gateway run --force`), then placed real Teams 1:1 and group calls (voice + screen-share), an outbound "call me back", and a chat-governance session. - **Evidence after fix (screenshots, recording, redacted logs):** Captured from real Microsoft Teams calls against a production tenant (redacted): **1. Be-seen avatar — the bot publishes its avatar as a live video tile** **2. `show_to_caller` — the bot screenshots a page and displays it fullscreen on its own video tile mid-call** **3. Outbound "call me back" — the bot rings the caller on Teams** **4. Result delivery even to voicemail — Teams transcribes the call-back's spoken result** **5. Screen recording of a live call** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a961d8ce-22f8-44a9-ae28-b475393e3115 Plus the redacted gateway/worker logs + transcripts in the **Proof & live-test evidence** section below (voice ASR→agent→TTS round-trip, realtime session lifecycle, the three-call recording-gate refuse→open, outbound notify call-back lifecycle, background-task→Teams-chat delivery reading the real machine clock, audit `🧾` mirror, and DLP `[REDACTED:email]` on the streamed wire path). - **Observed result after fix:** Multi-turn round-trips with audible replies; `look_at_screen` answered across two screen-share windows incl. a content switch + retroactive history; the avatar published a live video tile; the outbound call-back spoke the result only after answer then auto-hung-up cleanly with no leaked state; the on-hangup recap + minutes landed in the caller's Teams chat as a real `.docx`; DLP redacted a secret on the live streamed reply. - **What was not tested:** Voice-message transcription (the tenant exposed no bot-deliverable voice clip; the graceful no-media path was observed), the DLP `secret` category on the wire (the model refused to repeat a dummy key; the `email` category exercises the same path), and the group-chat form of the message action — each is unit-tested instead. - **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Private tenant identifiers (AAD object ids, call ids, endpoints) are redacted, and some evidence is redacted runtime logs rather than recordings; the avatar is rendered by the companion media worker (separate repo), so its screenshot is the viewable artifact for the CVI rendering drivers this PR emits. - **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** The pre-fix `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` default-off self-answer bug was reproduced before the fix (a speakerphone echo made the bot re-greet itself in a loop); fixed by defaulting it on plus the playout-clock echo guard. ## Proof & live-test evidence All evidence below is from real Microsoft Teams calls against a production tenant (private identifiers redacted). ![OpenClaw companion worker rendering the animated "jarvis" avatar live on the bot's Teams video tile during a production-tenant validation call](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/06af3f1f-b943-4a40-88c9-cd4eb95fc7ba) *Avatar shown is the worker-side `AvatarImagePath=jarvis` Lottie loop with speech-reactive zoom (consuming this PR's `expression` / `speech.marks` viseme / `display.image` rendering drivers); the default `AvatarImagePath=avatar` SVG style adds full lip-sync viseme mouth shapes, expressions, and blink.* ### Voice / realtime **Full inbound audio round-trip on a real multi-turn Teams call** — caller speech → ElevenLabs STT → agent (`microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`) → ElevenLabs TTS, across multiple turns (~70 s). Disfluencies in the transcript confirm live ASR; `spoken` payloads are exactly what was synthesized back to the caller. ``` user (STT): "How are you, man?" asst (spoken): "I'm doing well, man - thanks. What can I help you with?" user (STT): "Tell me more about you." asst (spoken): "I'm your phone assistant in OpenClaw - calm, practical, here to help..." ```
Gateway lifecycle log (streaming path, provider msteams) ``` MsteamsMediaStream listening port=8443 path=/voice/msteams/stream [voice-call] msteams realtime transcription provider: elevenlabs [voice-call] msteams TTS provider configured MsteamsMediaStream: connection open MsteamsProvider: session.start callId= threadId= caller.aadId= MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed ```
**Speech-to-speech realtime mode on a real call** — operator placed a Teams call, spoke several turns, and heard the OpenAI-realtime bot reply; clean session lifecycle, 0 errors. **Verbal interrupts / barge-in on the ~7.5 min inbound 1:1 validation call** — deterministic verbal interrupts in English, by wake-phrase, and in Arabic ("توقف"); bilingual mirroring + translation; consult with the "thinking" avatar expression across six tool runs. ### Vision & CVI **`look_at_screen` and `show_to_caller` on the same validation call** — `look_at_screen` handled a scene change plus retroactive keyframe history across two screen-share windows; `show_to_caller` rendered a captioned PiP slideshow at exactly the coded hold times (4500 ms non-final / 5000 ms final), verified in the worker log. The avatar screenshot above is the live consumer of the `expression` / `speech.marks` viseme / `display.image` drivers this PR emits. ### Outbound call-back **Outbound "call me when it's done" notify call-back on a real call**, placed by an in-call background task: `placed → Established → recording-gate open → result spoken only after answer → notify-mode auto-hangup after audio drain` — complete, un-clipped delivery with clean teardown. **`openclaw_agent_task` background task → Teams chat delivery on a real call.** Caller said *"take care of local time in the background and ping me on Teams."* The realtime model invoked `openclaw_agent_task`, spoke the ack, the caller hung up, the agent ran in the background, and the result landed in the caller's Teams chat — reading the real machine clock, not a canned reply: ``` > I'm checking the local machine time now and will send it here shortly. > Local machine time: 2026-06-04 05:28:13 +00:00. ``` ### Meeting / chat / governance **Recording-status gate (Microsoft Media Access API) observed refusing, then opening, across real calls.** With `requireRecordingStatus: true` (default), delegation is refused until the external worker signals recording active (the caller audibly hears a "recording isn't active" reply), then the gate opens and `openclaw_agent_consult` runs.
Redacted gateway log across real calls (recording gate) ``` # Call A — no recording signal → consult refused MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=0c005180… caller.aadId= MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed 0c005180… # Call B — worker reports recording active → gate opens → consult runs MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=2d005480… caller.aadId= MsteamsProvider: recording.status 2d005480… = active (realtime) <-- gate opens ```
**Governance on the wire** — the audit-channel mirror produced a `🧾` line per streamed DM reply (loop guard holding for the bot's own reply inside the audit chat), and DLP rendered `[REDACTED:email]` in the Teams client on the **streamed** path. On-hangup recap plus on-demand minutes both landed in chat. ### Message action — "Ask OpenClaw about this" The Teams message-context command quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path; on the 2026-06-12 governance session it **dispatched and replied** in the Teams client. The invoke-dispatch path was merged with upstream's adaptive-card value helpers in `monitor-handler.ts` (both kept), with message-action extraction unit-covered. (The group-chat form is unit-tested, not live-tested in this tenant.) ### Setup / upgrade compatibility (fresh-start & upgrade path) **Everything here is additive and backward-compatible — no data-model migration is required** (this is the upgrade-path evidence for the compatibility / "serialized state" flags): - **All new behavior is OPT-IN config.** The `msteams` voice provider and every governance feature are off unless explicitly configured: `msteams.meetingRecap`, `msteams.bilingual`, `channels.msteams.transcribeVoiceMessages`, `channels.msteams.auditChannel`, `channels.msteams.dlp.enabled`, plus the `msteams.groupCall` / `msteams.outbound` blocks. A config that doesn't set these behaves exactly as before. - **Existing voice-call providers are unaffected.** `twilio` / `telnyx` / `plivo` / `mock` route and behave unchanged; `msteams` is a new enum case resolved only when selected, on its own authenticated bridge listener (`msteams.port`/`path`), not the public webhook plane. - **plugin-sdk additions are optional with a no-op facade.** `RealtimeVoiceImageInput` / `sendImage` and the TTS alignment surface are optional contract additions; realtime providers that don't implement them get a no-op facade — no behavior change for non-Teams voice. - **No persisted-state migration.** The files flagged as "serialized state" are **test fixtures** (`elevenlabs/*.test.ts`, `openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`) and **in-memory per-call state** (`msteams-realtime.ts`, `session-runtime.ts`); nothing persisted crosses versions. `bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` is **regenerated tooling output** (the validation schema for the new opt-in keys), not user data — old configs validate as before. - **Two flagged default changes only**, both fixing live-reproduced bugs and both reversible: `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` off → on (self-answer echo fix; set `false` to restore), and outbound `defaultMode` defaults to `notify` + speak-only-on-answer (set `"conversation"` to restore). **New real `.docx` minutes.** End-of-meeting minutes are now a real OOXML Word document with **deterministic per-speaker attribution** ("Sara: …") from unmixed meeting audio, delivered to chat alongside the on-hangup recap. Unit coverage: the voice-call suite is **610 passing**, with dedicated suites for the docx minutes, welcome-card DLP, recording gate, interrupt matcher (incl. Arabic), and DLP cards. ## Acknowledgements Contributed by the team at the **Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC)** — Dubai, United Arab Emirates — bringing Microsoft Teams voice & video (the Conversational Video Interface) to OpenClaw for public-sector use. We welcome the maintainers' guidance on shaping it for the wider community.

target.title: Allow WhatsApp observe-only message hooks
target.body:
## Summary
- let WhatsApp disabled DM/group policy paths become observe-only when `pluginHooks.messageReceived=true`
- keep observe-only messages unread by returning `shouldMarkRead=false`
- stop observe-only messages immediately after message_received hooks fire, before reply dispatch/session side effects

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: WhatsApp currently drops disabled DM/group messages before the `message_received` hooks can feed observe-only Comms triage. This change lets disabled-policy messages become observe-only when the hook is explicitly enabled, while keeping replies and read receipts off.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `dex/whatsapp-observe-only-hooks`, commit `d11e891a`, using the real WhatsApp extension source and OpenClaw test/runtime tooling.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-whatsapp.config.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts`
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 /Users/chai/.openclaw/workspace/projects/openclaw-source

Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  25 passed (25)
  Start at  21:06:01
  Duration  14.16s (transform 11.12s, setup 369ms, import 13.11s, tests 569ms, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
```

- Observed result after fix: The after-fix WhatsApp extension behavior now covers disabled DM and disabled group messages as observe-only admissions with `shouldMarkRead=false`, emits `message_received` hooks, and returns before reply dispatch/background reply side effects.
- What was not tested: A live inbound WhatsApp message through an officially released trusted `@openclaw/whatsapp` package was not tested because this PR is the upstream source patch required before that trusted package can be released.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Live Comms ingest remains intentionally blocked until this patch lands in the official trusted WhatsApp package and James updates/reinstalls that package. No installed OpenClaw package files were hot-patched for this proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): On the current official trusted package, disabled WhatsApp DM/group messages are blocked before the hook, so Comms stays empty; that is why this PR changes the source package rather than the local installed artifact.

## Safety
- preserves disabled DM/group reply policy
- does not add any outbound Comms send path
- does not mark observe-only messages read

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-whatsapp.config.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions`

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: fix(googlechat): sanitize internal tool-trace lines from outbound text (#90684)
target.body:
## Summary

- Google Chat outbound text only ran `sanitizeForPlainText()`, so internal assistant trace lines leaked to users — most visibly the `⚠️ 🛠️ \`<command> (agent)\` failed` banner produced for a benign non-zero shell exit (e.g. a `grep` that finds no match → exit 1). The turn completes successfully, but the user gets an alarming "failed" message.
- Fix: run `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText()` before `sanitizeForPlainText()` in the googlechat outbound `sanitizeText` hook — the same composition Discord (`sanitizeDiscordFrontChannelText`) and WhatsApp (`normalizeWhatsAppPayloadText`) already use. The hook runs pre-chunking in the shared deliver path, so it covers every text send (replies, sub-agent announces, cron deliveries).
- Out of scope: this does NOT close the broader cross-channel umbrella (#90684) for Telegram/Feishu/Slack/msteams, and it does not change the Codex-native warning taxonomy (#88332). It is one focused Google Chat instance.
- Success: a benign non-zero shell exit no longer emits a user-visible `⚠️ 🛠️ … failed` banner in Google Chat; ordinary prose is untouched.

## Linked context

Closes part of #90684 (Google Chat instance of the non-Discord assistant-visible sanitizer gap).
Related #88332, #87610.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Google Chat leaked the internal `⚠️ 🛠️ \`<command> (agent)\` failed` trace banner (and similar trace lines) to end users because outbound text skipped `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText()`. Reproduced live: a front-desk agent answered a user correctly, but the channel also posted a standalone bot message `⚠️ 🛠️ search "…" in ~/.openclaw/workspace-* (agent) failed` from a `grep` that exited 1 (no match).
- Real environment tested: Production OpenClaw `2026.6.8` gateway running the installed `@openclaw/googlechat` plugin with this patch applied to the live adapter, on a real Google Chat deployment. `/health` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: In the deployed plugin's own module context, ran `node` to exercise the patched outbound `sanitizeText` composition on the exact leaked banner and on ordinary prose, then scanned trajectories captured after the patch went live for the trigger condition (benign non-zero tool exits):

```
# 1) deployed outbound sanitize composition (exactly what the patched hook runs)
node -e 'import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-chunking").then(async tc => {
  const { sanitizeForPlainText } = await import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-outbound");
  const hook = t => sanitizeForPlainText(tc.sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(t));
  console.log("OUT:", JSON.stringify(hook(
    "Listo.\n⚠️ 🛠️ `search \"Pipeline\" in ~/.openclaw/workspace-* (agent)` failed")));
  console.log("normal:", JSON.stringify(hook("El pipeline tiene 3 deals.")));
})'

# 2) did the trigger condition actually occur in live traffic since deploy?
node scan-trajectories.mjs   # counts tool failures / exit-1 captured after deploy
```

- Evidence after fix: copied live output / console output (stdout) from the patched `2026.6.8` deployment, captured with `node` against the live plugin module:

```
OUT: "Listo."
normal: "El pipeline tiene 3 deals."

trigger-events (tool fail / exit 1) after deploy: 11  (across 2 agents)
banner present in delivered/outbound payload after deploy: 0
```

- Observed result after fix: the patched `node` hook removes the `⚠️ 🛠️ … (agent) failed` banner line and returns only the real assistant prose (`"Listo."`); ordinary text passes through unchanged. Over ~16h of live traffic the benign-exit trigger fired 11 times and zero failed-tool banners reached the channel. No recurrence reported by the user who originally hit it.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a screenshot of the Google Chat client UI itself (proof is copied live `node`/stdout output from the patched deployment, not a client screen recording). I did not exercise the broader Telegram/Feishu/Slack/msteams paths (out of scope; tracked by #90684).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: importing the full `channel.adapters` ESM module standalone fails outside the gateway process (runtime-context deps), so the after-fix proof exercises the exact same two-function composition the patched hook runs (`sanitizeForPlainText(sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(text))`) resolved from the live plugin's own module context, rather than re-instantiating the whole adapter object. Space IDs / account IDs / user details are intentionally omitted.
- Before evidence: pre-patch, the identical banner reached the user as a standalone bot message (`⚠️ 🛠️ search "…" in ~/.openclaw/workspace-* (agent) failed`) while the actual answer was delivered fine; on the same runtime `node` shows `sanitizeForPlainText(text)` alone returns the banner unchanged, whereas `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(text)` strips it.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts -t "sanitizeText"` → 2 passed (new `googlechatPlugin outbound sanitizeText` describe block): asserts the `(agent) failed` banner is stripped via the real `googlechatOutboundAdapter.base.sanitizeText` hook, and that ordinary prose is preserved.
- Regression coverage added in `channel.test.ts`. CI/unit tests are supplemental; the after-fix proof above is copied live output from a real deployment.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — internal trace/failure banners are no longer shown in Google Chat; legitimate prose is unaffected.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No — this narrows user-visible internal-trace leakage only; no deps/workflows/credentials/permissions touched.
- Highest-risk area: over-stripping legitimate content. Mitigated by reusing the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText()` (already used by Discord/WhatsApp) rather than new regexes, plus the new unit test asserting ordinary prose is preserved.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review. Structured after-fix real-behavior proof from a live patched deployment added above (addressing the ClawSweeper proof blocker).
- Waiting on: maintainer review. The unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents-core-tools` shard failure does not touch `extensions/googlechat`.
- Bot comments addressed: ClawSweeper "needs real behavior proof before merge" — added structured after-fix evidence and observed result from a real Google Chat deployment.

AI-assisted change.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): add listGroupsLive for real-time group directory
target.body:
## Root Cause

The WhatsApp channel directory adapter only registered `listGroups` (which reads from the config map `channels.whatsapp.groups`) but did **not** register `listGroupsLive`. The CLI (and other consumers) prefer `listGroupsLive` when available, so users who have active WhatsApp group memberships but an empty config-based groups map would see an empty directory. No `listGroupsLive` implementation existed for WhatsApp.

## Summary

- Bug: WhatsApp directory `/groups` returned empty even when the account is a member of groups, because only the config-backed `listGroups` was wired up but not the live-fetch variant.
- This PR adds `listWhatsAppDirectoryGroupsLive` that fetches groups from the Baileys socket via `groupFetchAllParticipating()`, with graceful fallback to config when the socket is unavailable or throws. The new function is registered in the channel directory adapter.
- Fixes #76722

## Revision (2026-06-20)

- Fixed a P2 issue where live groups were filtered by JID only, so `--query "Family"` would not match a group named "Family Chat". Now builds entries first and filters by both JID and subject name (matching Discord/Slack live directory behavior).
- Commit: `e7ac40ee0e`

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/directory-config.test.ts` — 8/8 passed (6 new tests + 2 existing; +1 test for name-based filtering)
- `pnpm tsgo:test` — passed (type check)
- `pnpm lint:extensions:bundled` — passed
- Autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- CI: The `checks-node-core-tooling` failure is an infrastructure/flaky issue unrelated to this PR (only touches `extensions/whatsapp/`)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp directory `/groups` now returns the account's real group memberships fetched live from the WhatsApp connection, filtered by both JID and group name.
Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22, branch `fix/issue-76722-whatsapp-directory-groups-live` commit `e7ac40ee0e`
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/directory-config.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test
pnpm lint:extensions:bundled
```
Evidence after fix:
```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/directory-config.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
   Start at  00:50:52
   Duration  25.63s
```
Observed result after fix: All 8 tests pass including the new test for name-based query filtering. Type check and lint pass cleanly.
What was not tested: Live E2E with a real WhatsApp account (requires Crabbox with WhatsApp auth); no WhatsApp directory binary/E2E test infrastructure exists.

target.title: fix(xai): respect ssrfPolicy and request.allowPrivateNetwork in image_generate
target.body:
## Summary


- **Problem**: `extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts` hardcodes `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: () => false`, blocking xAI image generation when the user configures a private-network gateway (e.g., `http://10.0.0.10:8090`). The SSRF guard fires regardless of `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork` or `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork`.
- **Solution**: Replace the hardcoded `false` with config-aware logic that reads `browser.ssrfPolicy` (via `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled`) and `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, matching the pattern already used by the OpenAI image provider (#63095).
- **What changed**: One file — `extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts` — +1 import, +1 logic line.
- **What did NOT change**: Other providers with the same hardcoded pattern (e.g., `extensions/deepinfra/`), xAI chat runtime, xAI auth flow, or any core SSRF logic. Scoped strictly to xAI image generation per issue scope.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #84627 (scoped to xAI image generation only; other providers with same pattern not fixed)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The xAI image provider's `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork` was hardcoded to `false`, ignoring both `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork` and `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork` config. Users with private-network xAI gateways (multi-tenant proxies, internal endpoints) are blocked from using xAI image generation even when they've explicitly opted in via config. The OpenAI equivalent was fixed in #63095; this applies the same fix to xAI.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: xAI `image_generate` blocked by SSRF when using a private-network base URL, despite user config opting in.
- **Real environment tested**: Unit tests (8 tests, all passing) + code review. The tests cover default deny, browser.ssrfPolicy opt-in, and provider request.allowPrivateNetwork opt-in paths. The fix follows the identical pattern proven in production since #63095 (OpenAI).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.test.ts --run
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.test.ts --run
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts

   RUN  v4.1.7

   ✓ |extension-providers| extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.test.ts (8 tests) 315ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  8 passed (8)
     Start at  09:54:27
     Duration  1.70s

  3 new SSRF tests:
  ✓ defaults allowPrivateNetwork to false when no SSRF opt-in is configured
  ✓ allows private network when browser.ssrfPolicy opts in
  ✓ allows private network when xai provider request.allowPrivateNetwork is set
  ```
  Unit test coverage showing the three SSRF decision paths:
  ```typescript
  // Test 1: Default deny - no config → allowPrivateNetwork: false
  expect(httpParams?.allowPrivateNetwork).toBe(false);

  // Test 2: browser.ssrfPolicy opt-in → isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled returns true → allowPrivateNetwork: true
  isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabledMock.mockReturnValueOnce(true);
  expect(httpParams?.allowPrivateNetwork).toBe(true);

  // Test 3: xai provider request.allowPrivateNetwork → even when isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled false → true
  isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabledMock.mockReturnValueOnce(false);
  expect(httpParams?.allowPrivateNetwork).toBe(true);
  ```
  The code diff (same pattern as OpenAI #63095):
  ```console
  $ git diff origin/main...HEAD -- extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  diff --git a/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts b/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  index b1d7c05ef..970db3528 100644
  --- a/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  +++ b/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  @@ -12,6 +12,7 @@ import {
     normalizeOptionalLowercaseString,
     normalizeOptionalString,
   } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/string-coerce-runtime";
  +import { isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime";
   import { XAI_BASE_URL, XAI_DEFAULT_IMAGE_MODEL, XAI_IMAGE_MODELS } from "./model-definitions.js";

   const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000;
  @@ -106,7 +107,9 @@ export function buildXaiImageGenerationProvider(): ImageGenerationProvider {
       },
       defaultBaseUrl: XAI_BASE_URL,
       resolveBaseUrl: ({ req }) => resolveXaiImageBaseUrl(req),
  -    resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: () => false,
  +    resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: ({ req }) =>
  +      isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled(req.cfg?.browser?.ssrfPolicy) ||
  +      Boolean(req.cfg?.models?.providers?.xai?.request?.allowPrivateNetwork),
       defaultTimeoutMs: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS,
       buildGenerateRequest: ({ req, inputImages, model, count }) => ({
         kind: "json",
  ```
  The `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled` function (shared, tested in `src/plugin-sdk/ssrf-policy.test.ts`):
  ```console
  $ node -e "
  const f = require('./dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime.js').isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled;
  console.log('Default:', f(undefined));
  console.log('Opted in:', f({dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: true}));
  "
  Default: false
  Opted in: true
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All 8 tests pass. The three SSRF-specific tests verify:
  1. Default (no config) → `allowPrivateNetwork: false` (default deny preserved) ✓
  2. `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: true` → `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled` → `allowPrivateNetwork: true` ✓
  3. `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` → `allowPrivateNetwork: true` (independent opt-in path) ✓
  The fix matches the identical pattern proven in the OpenAI provider (#63095). `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled` is the same function already used by OpenAI and covered by shared unit tests.
- **What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real private-network xAI gateway (requires xAI API key + private gateway access — unavailable in this environment). Other providers with the same hardcoded pattern (`extensions/deepinfra/`). xAI chat runtime (unrelated).

## Root Cause

`extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts:109` — `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: () => false` was copied from the initial provider scaffold and never updated to respect user config.

## Regression Test Plan

Added three SSRF opt-in tests in `extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.test.ts`:
1. Default deny (no opt-in config → `allowPrivateNetwork: false`)
2. browser.ssrfPolicy opt-in → `allowPrivateNetwork: true`
3. xai provider request.allowPrivateNetwork opt-in → `allowPrivateNetwork: true`

All 8 tests pass. The existing `src/plugin-sdk/ssrf-policy.test.ts` suite covers `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled` itself.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who have configured `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: true` or `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` will no longer get SSRF-blocked when using xAI image generation with a private-network base URL.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No — opt-in only, same as OpenAI already allows
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No — only changes the SSRF guard decision
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Branch: `fix/xai-ssrf-allow-private-network-84627`
- Base: `origin/main` (44e6caff5)

### Steps

1. Configure `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: true` in `openclaw.json`
2. Set `models.providers.xai.baseUrl` to a private-network address
3. Run `image_generate` with xAI model

### Expected

Image generation succeeds; no SSRF block.

### Actual (before fix)

```
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://10.0.0.10:8090
  reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[image-generation] candidate failed: xai/grok-imagine-image: Blocked hostname...
```

## Verification

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Three SSRF opt-in tests (default deny, browser policy, provider config) + 5 pre-existing tests = 8 total passing. Both config paths independently tested.
- **Edge cases checked**: Default deny when neither config is set; browser policy opt-in alone; provider config opt-in alone when browser policy returns false.
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live E2E with real xAI private gateway (requires xAI API key + private gateway). Other providers with identical hardcoded pattern (`extensions/deepinfra/`).

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — default behavior unchanged (both config checks return `false` when unset)
- [x] Config/env changes? No — no new config keys
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: "fix #84627 — xAI image_generate blocked by SSRF — resolveAllowPrivateNetwork hardcoded to false in extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts"

## Risks and Mitigations

None. This is a strict superset of the existing logic — when neither config is set, it returns `false` (same as the hardcoded default). The same pattern has been proven in production since #63095 (OpenAI).

---

Related to #84627

target.title: fix(memory-core): report active dreaming phases in status
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `memory status` so it reports active light-only and REM-only dreaming instead of deriving the `Dreaming` line solely from the deep promotion config.
- Keep the existing deep-only status detail intact, including the current promotion thresholds and limits, to avoid the compatibility regression that blocked the prior replacement PR.
- Add focused CLI regression coverage for light-only, REM-only, and deep-detail-preservation status output.

What problem does this PR solve?
`openclaw memory status` could print `Dreaming: off` when global dreaming was enabled but only the light or REM phase was active.

Why does this matter now?
`#67868` is still open, source-reproducible on current `main`, and the last replacement PR was self-closed after ClawSweeper flagged missing REM coverage and dropped deep diagnostics.

What is the intended outcome?
Operators should see truthful phase-aware dreaming status without losing the existing deep promotion detail they already rely on.

What is intentionally out of scope?
No scheduler, config-schema, or dreaming execution changes. This only touches status formatting and regression coverage.

What does success look like?
Light-only and REM-only configs show active dreaming in `memory status`, deep-only configs keep their detailed thresholds, and the focused memory-core suites stay green.

What should reviewers focus on?
The `formatDreamingSummary` contract in `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts` and the new regression cases in `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #67868

Related #92979
Related #67910
Related #67923
Related #67882

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
No direct maintainer request. This is a replacement for previously closed attempts on the same issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  `memory status` incorrectly printed `Dreaming: off` for light-only and REM-only dreaming configs.
- Real environment tested:
  Local `main` checkout on macOS with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temp workspace, and temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  `HOME="$HOME_DIR" OPENCLAW_HOME="$HOME_DIR/.openclaw" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$STATE_DIR" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$TMPROOT/light.json" pnpm openclaw memory status --agent main`
  `HOME="$HOME_DIR" OPENCLAW_HOME="$HOME_DIR/.openclaw" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$STATE_DIR" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$TMPROOT/rem.json" pnpm openclaw memory status --agent main`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
LIGHT
Memory Search (main)
Provider: openai (requested: openai)
Model: openai
Sources: memory
Indexed: 0/0 files · 0 chunks
Dirty: yes
Store: /tmp/openclaw-memory-status-proof.1QCdHp/state/memory/main.sqlite
Workspace: /tmp/openclaw-memory-status-proof.1QCdHp/workspace
Dreaming: light=5 * * * * (UTC) · limit=4 · lookbackDays=2
```

```text
REM
Memory Search (main)
Provider: openai (requested: openai)
Model: openai
Sources: memory
Indexed: 0/0 files · 0 chunks
Dirty: yes
Store: /tmp/openclaw-memory-status-proof.1QCdHp/state/memory/main.sqlite
Workspace: /tmp/openclaw-memory-status-proof.1QCdHp/workspace
Dreaming: rem=0 6 * * 0 (UTC) · limit=3 · lookbackDays=9 · minPatternStrength=0.81
```

- Observed result after fix:
  Both isolated runs now show active dreaming with the expected phase-specific summary instead of `Dreaming: off`.
- What was not tested:
  No live background cron sweep or Dream Diary execution was run; this PR does not change dreaming execution.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  The proof uses isolated temporary state rather than an existing long-lived gateway workspace, because the issue is a status-formatting bug and the formatter is fully config-driven.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  Focused Vitest coverage on current main failed with `Dreaming: off` for both light-only and REM-only configs before this patch.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts -t "dreaming"`
- `pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
Added status assertions for light-only, REM-only, and deep-detail-preservation cases in `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`.

What failed before this fix, if known?
The new light-only and REM-only status tests failed on current main because the formatter still returned `Dreaming: off`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No

What is the highest-risk area?
The `memory status` text contract for deep-only dreaming output.

How is that risk mitigated?
The formatter keeps the existing deep-only detail, and the new regression test asserts those deep diagnostics still appear.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
Maintainer review plus normal CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
CI has not run yet on GitHub. Author-side proof and focused tests are included above.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
This replacement explicitly addresses the gaps called out on #92979: REM coverage, preserved deep diagnostics, and real `memory status` output.

target.title: fix(compaction): emit after_compaction on no-op and use JSON-safe validator delimiters (#81925)
target.body:
## Summary

OpenClaw issue #81925 documents two coupled defects that surface as HTTP 500s on voice channels when the context engine runs under memory pressure, especially for open-weight tool-calling models:

1. `after_compaction` is never emitted when compaction is a legitimate no-op (`{ok:true, compacted:false}`), so plugin observers that pair `before_compaction`/`after_compaction` wait forever and time out.
2. Schema-validation error messages wrap property names in single quotes (`'sessionTarget'`). Models trained on JSON tool calls copy the trailing `'` delimiter back into their next attempt and get stuck in an unrecoverable retry loop.

This change fixes both, on every surface where they occur.

## Root cause

### Bug 1: after_compaction not emitted on no-op compaction

`before_compaction` fires unconditionally once the engine owns compaction, but `after_compaction` was gated on `result.compacted === true` on **three** independent compaction surfaces:

- `compact.queued.ts` (engine-owned queued path): the after-hook block required `result.ok && result.compacted`.
- `compact.ts` `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirect` (native direct path): the `!containsRealConversationMessages(...)` early return sent `{ok:true, compacted:false, reason:"no real conversation messages"}` straight back to the caller, after `before_compaction` already fired, without ever calling `runAfterCompactionHooks`.
- `run.ts` `runOwnsCompactionAfterHook` (timeout-recovery and overflow-recovery paths): paired with `runOwnsCompactionBeforeHook`, which fires `before_compaction` unconditionally, but the after-hook returned early on `!compactResult.compacted`.

So a paired observer sees `before` with no matching `after`. The voice plugin waits out its 3s timeout, falls back to `tool-result-truncation`, and only recovers after ~3 attempts (~2.5 min), long after the client has already 500'd.

### Bug 2: validator delimiters trip tool callers

Validation error formatting wrapped property names in single quotes in two places:

- `packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.ts` `formatValidationErrors`: `unexpected property '<name>'` and `must have required property '<name>'` (gateway RPC params + plugin session action result validation, the surface an LLM-driven action call hits).
- `src/plugins/schema-validator.ts` `formatRequiredMessage`: `must have required property '<name>'` (plugin session action **payload** validation, the surface an LLM-submitted payload hits). Its sibling `additionalProperties` message was already double-quoted, so this was the lone single-quote holdout.

A model that parses the error and copies the property token captures `<name>'` (with the stray quote), re-emits the same malformed key, and re-triggers the identical error.

## Fix

- **Bug 1, all three surfaces:** emit `after_compaction` whenever compaction completes (`result.ok`), with `compactedCount: 0` and a `reason` discriminator on the no-op path. The engine paths forward the engine's own skip reason (default `policy_skipped`); the native path reports `no_real_conversation_messages`. Added an optional `reason?: string` to the `after_compaction` hook contract (`PluginHookAfterCompactionEvent`, `CompactionHookRunner`, `runAfterCompactionHooks`), additive and backward compatible, so existing subscribers are unaffected while observers can now distinguish a real compaction from a skip without fabricating synthetic `N->N` events. The `!compactResult.ok` guard is preserved, so genuine failures/aborts still do not emit a success `after_compaction`.
- **Bug 2, both surfaces:** use JSON-compliant double quotes (`"<name>"`) for `unexpected property` and `must have required property` in `formatValidationErrors`, and for `must have required property` in `schema-validator`. All four property-name delimiters across both validators are now consistently double-quoted, matching JSON tool-call syntax so there is no stray delimiter to copy.

## Why this covers the whole issue (not one surface)

- The no-op `after_compaction` gap existed on **three** independent compaction code paths (engine-owned queued, native direct, and timeout/overflow recovery in the run loop). Fixing only one would still hang on the others. All three are fixed and all three have regression coverage.
- The single-quote delimiter existed on **two** independent validator paths (gateway-protocol result validation and plugins payload validation). An LLM tool caller hits the payload path; fixing only the result path would leave the primary tool-caller surface broken. Both are fixed.
- The downstream symptoms the issue describes (synthetic `N->N` events, `tool-result-truncation` fallback after ~3 attempts, client 500s) all stem from the missing real event; emitting it removes the need for the synthetic-event workaround at the root.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.timeout-triggered-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> 136 passed (adds 5 regression tests: engine-skip no-op, engine default reason, native-skip paired-observer, timeout-recovery no-op, overflow-recovery no-op).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts src/plugins/config-schema.test.ts src/channels/plugins/config-schema.test.ts src/plugins/schema-validator.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.default-token.test.ts src/gateway/client.test.ts` -> all passed (adds a JSON-double-quote validator assertion).
- `tsgo:test` type-check clean over the changed files; `oxlint --type-aware` 0 findings; `oxfmt --check` clean.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: a no-op compaction now emits `after_compaction` (so a paired voice observer is not left waiting), and the gateway-protocol validator now quotes property names with JSON double quotes (so a tool-calling model cannot copy a stray delimiter).

Real environment tested: the actual built-from-source OpenClaw runtime functions in this branch, driven directly through `node` (via the `tsx` ESM loader) outside any harness. Bug 1 drives the real `containsRealConversationMessages`, `runBeforeCompactionHooks`, and `runAfterCompactionHooks` from `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/`, with a real `registerInternalHook` voice-style observer that increments on `before_compaction` and decrements on `after_compaction`. Bug 2 drives the real `validateTalkClientCreateParams` + `formatValidationErrors` from `packages/gateway-protocol/`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: register a paired `session:compact:before` / `session:compact:after` observer, feed a heartbeat-only transcript (so `containsRealConversationMessages` returns false, the real no-op trigger), then run the real before/after emission the patched no-op branch performs; separately, submit a Talk client-create call carrying an unexpected `sessionTarget` property to the real validator and read back the formatted message, then extract the property token a JSON model would copy.

Evidence after fix (live console output from `node` driving the real runtime functions):

```
$ node --import tsx proof-bug1.mts   # drives real compaction hook emission
containsRealConversationMessages(heartbeat) = false

# BEFORE the fix (no-op fires before_compaction only):
  observer pending balance = 1 <- stuck, after_compaction never arrives (hang)
  observer received after  = null

# AFTER the fix (no-op also fires after_compaction):
  observer pending balance = 0 <- balanced
  observer received after  = {"compactedCount":0,"reason":"no_real_conversation_messages"}

$ node --import tsx proof-bug2.mts   # drives real gateway-protocol validator
validator.ok           = false
validator.message      = at root: unexpected property "sessionTarget"
token a model copies   = "sessionTarget"
copies as a clean JSON key? = true
```

Observed result after fix: the real no-op path now delivers `after_compaction` with `{compactedCount:0, reason:"no_real_conversation_messages"}` to the paired observer, returning its pending balance to 0 instead of hanging at 1; the real validator emits `unexpected property "sessionTarget"`, and the token a model copies back is a clean double-quoted JSON key.

What was not tested: a live end-to-end voice-channel session against a real 7B-14B llama-server under memory pressure. This change is at the compaction-hook and validation-formatter layers, exercised above by driving the real runtime functions plus the regression coverage; a full hardware voice loop was out of scope for this environment.

Fixes #81925

target.title: fix(ui): show coalesced update restarts
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a Control UI update feedback gap where `update.run` can return `ok: true` with `restart.coalesced`, but the UI treated that as a normal silent success.

This matters now because the gateway already exposes the in-progress restart signal, and hiding it makes the Update button appear idle even though an update restart is already pending.

The intended outcome is that users see an info banner telling them the update is waiting on an already in-progress gateway restart, while the existing reconnect verification still tracks the expected post-update version.

Out of scope: changing gateway restart scheduling, update handoff behavior, package update logic, or recovery policy.

Success looks like the Control UI preserving reconnect verification and surfacing the coalesced restart state instead of leaving the user with no visible feedback.

Reviewers should focus on the `runUpdate` success path and whether the banner state interacts correctly with existing post-update reconnect verification.

## Linked context

Closes #78481

Related: none beyond the linked issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. The issue is labeled queueable/source-repro/fix-shape-clear and has ClawSweeper guidance pointing to this UI/controller path.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `update.run` responses with `ok: true` and `restart.coalesced` no longer get treated as a silent success in the Control UI.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node 22.22.3.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/config.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest run passed 2 test files and 110 tests.
- Observed result after fix: the controller keeps `pendingUpdateExpectedVersion`, keeps managed-service handoff false, and sets an info banner when `restart: { coalesced: true }` is returned.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live browser update click against a real gateway with a stuck in-flight restart.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is source-level/controller-level through the real Control UI controller path, not a live gateway/browser reproduction.
- Before evidence: current main returned early on the `ok` update path before surfacing `restart.coalesced`, leaving the UI with no visible feedback for an already in-progress restart.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/config.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/controllers/config.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added a focused `runUpdate` regression test covering `restart: { coalesced: true }`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new regression would fail before this change because `runUpdate` returned from the successful update path without setting `updateStatusBanner`.

If no test was added, why not?

A regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The update success path in the Control UI.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change only reads existing gateway metadata and leaves restart scheduling unchanged. The regression test verifies that reconnect verification state is preserved while the banner is shown.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on maintainer review and CI. Live browser proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No PR bot/reviewer comments yet. This implements the ClawSweeper issue guidance on #78481.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve titles on main upsert
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves existing non-empty `label` and `displayName` during `upsertSessionEntry` replacement writes when the incoming runtime entry omits those fields.
- Keeps explicit replacement semantics intact: non-empty titles still replace previous titles, and explicit `null` / empty title metadata is still persisted.
- Adds focused regression coverage for omitted runtime title metadata, explicit empty metadata, explicit title replacement, and the existing patch/replace title-clear path.

## Main Target

- Targets `main` after `release/2026.6.8` was already released.
- Replaces #91690 with the same bug fix synchronized onto current `main` and a narrower compatibility-safe contract.

## Motivation

Runtime session refreshes can replace a stored session entry with metadata that does not include user-facing title fields. Before this patch, that replacement could drop stable title metadata even though the caller was only refreshing runtime state. The store boundary is the right place to protect omitted title fields because every replacement upsert passes through it.

## Compatibility

Compatibility risk is intentionally minimized. This patch only preserves title fields when the replacement entry does not own `label` / `displayName` at all. Callers that explicitly send non-empty, `null`, or empty string title values keep their current replacement behavior. Intentional deletion through `patchSessionEntry({ replaceEntry: true })` also remains covered.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/sessions.test.ts` - 54 passed
- `npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md src/config/sessions.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.ts`
- `npx oxlint src/config/sessions.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- GitHub checks on `3b6ab1d36f510c7bbbdad50bfd7fe4bb8d4c3719`: dependency/security/proof/label/dispatch/auto-response checks passed; unrelated superseded bot runs were cancelled or skipped by GitHub Actions concurrency/event filters.
## Privacy

The PR text and tests use only generic lane/session fixtures. No personal identifiers, account ids, or real session names are included.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Risk

- Session state: low, targeted store-boundary preservation for omitted fields only.
- Compatibility: low, explicit replacement and explicit clearing behavior remains unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: a replacement `upsertSessionEntry` runtime refresh that omits `label` / `displayName` preserves an existing stable user-facing title.
- Real environment tested: the original store-boundary proof was run on the predecessor PR head; the current main-based head `3b6ab1d36f510c7bbbdad50bfd7fe4bb8d4c3719` carries the same behavior patch synchronized onto `main` and passed focused store tests plus the GitHub Real behavior proof workflow.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval <redacted temp OpenClaw session-store proof>`
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the temporary OpenClaw session-store proof:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx --eval <redacted temp OpenClaw session-store proof>",
  "tempState": "redacted tmp OpenClaw state dir",
  "preservedAfterRuntimeRefresh": {
    "sessionId": "after-redacted",
    "label": "Stable Default Lane",
    "displayName": "Stable Default Lane"
  },
  "explicitEmptyReplacementStillPersists": {
    "label": null,
    "displayName": ""
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the runtime refresh advanced the stored session id while preserving the omitted title fields, and the explicit empty replacement case still persisted `null` / empty string title metadata.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI rendering was not exercised in this proof; this patch is limited to the session-store replacement boundary and is covered there.

target.title: fix #95378: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95378
target.body:
## Summary

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #95378. Direct Telegram replies for a shared session now build recent prompt context from the canonical session transcript when available, so replies can see fresher turns that arrived through another transport such as gateway/iOS instead of binding to stale Telegram-only cached context.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** A user could approve or answer an ambiguous prompt in Telegram after continuing the same OpenClaw session elsewhere, but Telegram previously supplied only its per-chat cache. That let the agent bind the reply to an older proposal and potentially execute the wrong action.
- **Fix classification:** Minimal canonical root-cause fix for shared direct-session prompt recency. Group, supergroup, forum-topic, mention-filter, and reply-target behavior remain on the existing Telegram chat/topic cache boundaries.
- **What did NOT change:** This PR does not change group, supergroup, forum-topic, mention-only, or reply-target scoping. It also does not alter transcript storage format, migration behavior, model routing, or non-Telegram prompt construction.

## What Problem This Solves

A direct Telegram reply in a shared OpenClaw session could be interpreted against stale Telegram-only conversation context even when the newest user/assistant turns were already present in the canonical session transcript through another transport. This PR makes direct Telegram prompt assembly use that shared transcript recency window before the current Telegram turn, preventing cross-channel replies from binding to an older proposal while keeping group/topic privacy boundaries unchanged.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root state/context invariant was split: Telegram direct prompt assembly read its account/chat/message cache because that cache is the Telegram transport source, while the canonical shared session state and transcript already held the newest gateway/iOS turns. Because the model-consumed direct prompt used the transport cache instead of the session state source-of-truth, a later Telegram reply could be resolved against an older Telegram-only proposal.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The direct-session context source now reads the canonical session transcript through the plugin SDK, filters it before the current Telegram turn, and keeps the existing prompt-reset timestamp boundary. That moves direct Telegram recency to the session state source-of-truth instead of merging raw per-transport caches, while preserving the Telegram scoped cache for groups/topics and as direct-session fallback when transcript context is unavailable.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The public contract surface is intentionally minimal: the new SDK export exposes bounded canonical session transcript text for plugin runtime use, with default behavior that returns no context when the session or transcript is absent. Compatibility is backward-safe because existing Telegram cache behavior remains the fallback and no schema/config migration or breaking data-format change is introduced. Related open PR / competition scan was treated as background only; this issue still needed the canonical direct-session root-cause fix. Out of scope: raw cache unification, new config knobs, and changing group/topic privacy semantics.

## Evidence

The focused regression command passed with exit code `0`, covering session transcript recency and Telegram prompt-context mapping. The committed diff whitespace check passed with exit code `0`. A full `pnpm build` was attempted; it reached the TypeScript/package build stages and plugin SDK export check successfully, then stopped because the local machine runs Node v22.17.0 while the repository requires Node v22.19+.

## Real behavior proof

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw worktree using the repository Vitest runner on Node v22.17.0 / pnpm 11.2.2. The tests exercise canonical transcript resolution/reading and Telegram prompt-context mapping without using a live Telegram bot token.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/session-transcript-context.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
  - `pnpm build`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Focused Vitest command exited `0`.
  - Rebased diff whitespace check exited `0`.
  - `pnpm build` reached `tsdown`, plugin SDK declaration generation, and `check-plugin-sdk-exports` successfully (`OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified`) before stopping in local startup metadata generation because this machine has Node v22.17.0 while the repo requires Node v22.19+.
- **Observed result after fix:** Transcript tests cover reading bounded user/assistant turns before the current Telegram message and resolving recent context from session identity. Telegram tests cover mapping a gateway-origin user turn plus assistant reply into chronological Telegram prompt messages, and existing Telegram message cache/dispatch tests continue to pass.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run an end-to-end live Telegram bot conversation or a full successful `pnpm build` on Node v22.19+; the local full build attempt was blocked by the Node version gate after the TypeScript/package export stages above.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/session-transcript-context.test.ts`, with existing Telegram coverage in `extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts` and `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression fixture records a gateway-origin user turn and assistant reply in the shared session transcript, then verifies a later Telegram direct prompt can include those prior session turns while excluding the current Telegram message by timestamp.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests exercise the invariant at the stable session-transcript and Telegram prompt-mapping seams without requiring a live Telegram token, so they lock the cross-transport recency contract while avoiding brittle network or provider dependencies.
- **Patch quality notes:** Adds a bounded reverse transcript reader, exports it through the plugin SDK, and maps entries in a small Telegram adapter. The catch/default path in Telegram intentionally logs transcript-read failure and falls back to the existing Telegram cache rather than masking a malformed prompt with mixed sources; default returns in the transcript reader represent absent sessions or non-message transcript lines. `expect.any(String)` is used only for generated transcript IDs.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The fix is focused, rebased onto current `origin/main`, covered by deterministic regression tests, and the remaining untested work is limited to a live Telegram credential path and a full build under Node v22.19+.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `impact:session-state`, `impact:message-loss`, `needs-product-decision`, direct-vs-group channel privacy boundaries.
- **Risk explanation:** Direct Telegram sessions now prefer canonical session transcript recency when transcript turns exist. This intentionally changes direct-session prompt context source-of-truth, but groups/supergroups/forum topics keep the existing Telegram cache path and topic/reply boundaries. If transcript lookup fails, direct Telegram falls back to the prior Telegram cache behavior.
- **Why acceptable:** The change is bounded to direct sessions, uses existing session identity/store resolution, honors current-turn and prompt-reset timestamps, maintains default backward compatibility for missing transcript state, and avoids introducing a new config knob or merging raw ingress-specific caches.
