target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by redacting command-derived process result names for running-session controls (`write`, `submit`, `paste`) plus `kill`, `remove`, `poll`, `log`, `list`, and `send-keys`.
- Added fake-secret regression coverage for `process write` and `process send-keys` command-derived `details.name` values.
- Final head verified locally: `24ac21f4280e9e403722c271d12fda0a4c68535d4`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache provider-normalized tool schema `parameters` for repeated embedded agent turns, but only when OpenClaw can identify one of its bundled SDK tool-compat normalizers.
- Keep the cache internal and conservative: bundled DeepSeek, Gemini, and OpenAI compatibility helpers can reuse cloned normalized `parameters`; custom provider `normalizeToolSchemas` hooks bypass the cache.
- Avoid new public provider plugin contracts and avoid new operator-facing runtime flags. Tool objects, execute closures, approval state, guards, and runtime policy are still rebuilt per turn.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated embedded agent turns can avoid redoing bundled provider tool-schema compatibility normalization when the provider/model/api/schema signature is unchanged. Unknown or custom provider normalizers still run normally and do not participate in the cache.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, current PR head `dee9bc5aa9e603be72a640b3483ae669ade3c29e`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a terminal proof with `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the production `normalizeProviderToolSchemas` entry. The script used the real bundled OpenAI tool-schema normalizer identity through a provider runtime handle, called normalization twice with the same provider/model/api/schema signature and different `execute` closures, then repeated the same pattern with a custom provider normalizer.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the current head:

```json
{
  "head": "dee9bc5aa9e603be72a640b3483ae669ade3c29e",
  "bundledCompat": {
    "afterFirst": { "bypass": 0, "hit": 0, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1 },
    "afterSecond": { "bypass": 0, "hit": 1, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1 },
    "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true,
    "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
    "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true
  },
  "customProvider": {
    "customNormalizeCalls": 2,
    "afterCustom": { "bypass": 2, "hit": 0, "miss": 0, "store": 0, "size": 0 },
    "customProviderBypassedCache": true
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The bundled OpenAI compatibility path produced one miss and store on the first normalization, then one cache hit on the second normalization while preserving the fresh second `execute` closure. The custom provider path bypassed the cache twice and invoked its normalizer twice, proving the cache remains internal to known bundled compatibility helpers.

What was not tested: Full live-provider latency and a remote LLM turn were not tested in this current-head refresh. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache complete tool inventory/materialization, policy resolution, prompt inputs, or full agent-turn startup.

## Additional validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 10.92s
Test files: 6 passed
Tests: 124 passed

pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files>
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check
exit 0
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Repeated embedded-agent turns reuse bundled provider tool-schema compatibility normalization for the same provider/model/api/schema signature, while non-bundled/custom provider normalizers still bypass the cache so plugin-specific behavior is not hidden.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout at PR head `b183b3fbe3379f870b8a65e5bf2fe43c8e5aa2ca`; production `normalizeProviderToolSchemas` entry point; real bundled OpenAI normalizer `normalizeOpenAIToolSchemas` via runtime handle; separate custom runtime handle for the non-bundled provider path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran this repo-local Node harness with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`; it imports `./src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts` and `./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.ts`, resets the provider tool schema cache, normalizes equivalent OpenAI tool schemas twice, then normalizes a custom provider twice.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "head": "b183b3fbe3379f870b8a65e5bf2fe43c8e5aa2ca",
  "bundledCompat": {
    "afterFirst": {
      "bypass": 0,
      "hit": 0,
      "miss": 1,
      "store": 1,
      "size": 1
    },
    "afterSecond": {
      "bypass": 0,
      "hit": 1,
      "miss": 1,
      "store": 1,
      "size": 1
    },
    "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true,
    "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
    "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true
  },
  "customProvider": {
    "customNormalizeCalls": 2,
    "afterCustom": {
      "bypass": 2,
      "hit": 0,
      "miss": 0,
      "store": 0,
      "size": 0
    },
    "customProviderBypassedCache": true
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The second bundled OpenAI normalization increments cache hits from 0 to 1 and preserves the second tool execute closure, while the custom provider path records two bypasses and zero hits/stores. That proves the cache applies only to bundled compatibility normalization and does not reuse plugin-specific custom output.
- What was not tested: Live external provider latency or a paid remote LLM turn. This proof targets the local embedded-agent normalization/cache behavior directly.

target.title: feat(lmstudio): opt-in idle TTL via native load API
target.body:
Title: feat(lmstudio): opt-in idle TTL via native load API

## Summary

- Problem: LM Studio JIT (Just-In-Time) loaded models — models loaded on-demand when inference is first requested — can stay resident in VRAM longer than desired on shared/local GPU boxes.
- Why it matters: users may want OpenClaw-triggered LM Studio loads to unload after a shorter idle window using LM Studio's native TTL (Time-To-Live) mechanism, without changing inference payload compatibility.
- What changed: `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds` now opts into passing `ttl` in the native LM Studio `POST /api/v1/models/load` body, alongside the existing `context_length`. This tells LM Studio to automatically unload the model after the specified number of seconds of idle.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): OpenAI-compatible inference requests are not mutated, `onPayload` TTL injection was removed, and OpenClaw does not force a default TTL when `ttlSeconds` is unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

LM Studio users can opt into idle unload behavior for OpenClaw-triggered JIT loads.

Add the following to your `openclaw.json` (find it with `openclaw config path`):

```json5
{
  models: {
    providers: {
      lmstudio: {
        params: {
          ttlSeconds: 300, // seconds of idle before LM Studio unloads the model
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

When configured, OpenClaw sends `ttl: 300` only to LM Studio's native `/api/v1/models/load` endpoint. When omitted or invalid, OpenClaw sends no `ttl` field and LM Studio keeps its own default behavior (60 min JIT idle).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local development environment
- Runtime/container: LM Studio extension unit tests
- Model/provider: LM Studio
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds`

### Steps

1. Configure LM Studio with `params.ttlSeconds`.
2. Trigger an LM Studio inference path that preloads a model.
3. Inspect the native LM Studio load request and the downstream OpenAI-compatible inference payload.

### Expected

- `/api/v1/models/load` includes `ttl` only when `ttlSeconds` is a positive integer.
- Inference payloads are unchanged and do not receive `ttl`.
- Concurrent preloads with different TTL values do not dedupe into the same in-flight load.

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

29 LM Studio tests passing, covering:

- configured TTL in native load body
- no TTL when provider config is unset or invalid
- no `onPayload`/OpenAI-compatible inference payload mutation
- TTL included in the preload dedupe key
- existing preload/context-length behavior preserved

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: reviewed the LM Studio load wrapper, model-load request construction, preload dedupe key, and docs update.
- Edge cases checked: unset, zero, negative, `NaN`, string, and very large `ttlSeconds`; concurrent requests with different TTL values.
- Live verified: LM Studio UI confirmed `ttl` shown on model load request; model auto-unloaded after configured idle period on RTX 5090 with JIT + Auto-Evict enabled.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; users who want shorter LM Studio idle unloads can add `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds`.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds` from config.
- Files/config to restore: LM Studio provider config only.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: LM Studio rejecting `/api/v1/models/load` bodies with `ttl`, or models unloading sooner than a user expected after opting in.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: LM Studio API behavior around `ttl` may vary by version.
  - Mitigation: TTL is opt-in and omitted unless explicitly configured.
- Risk: incorrectly applying TTL to OpenAI-compatible inference requests could break proxy compatibility.
  - Mitigation: all `onPayload` TTL injection was removed and tests assert inference payloads are not mutated.
## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: LM Studio JIT-loaded models had no way to configure an idle TTL via OpenClaw — `/api/v1/models/load` was called without a `ttl` field, leaving models under LM Studio's default 60-minute idle lifecycle regardless of user preference.

- **Real environment tested**: Local LM Studio server (0.3.x) on RTX 5090 Windows host (`10.0.0.91:1234`) with JIT loading and Auto-Evict enabled. Embedding model: `text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Added `"models": { "providers": { "lmstudio": { "params": { "ttlSeconds": 60 } } } }` to `openclaw.json`
  2. Triggered an LM Studio embedding warmup via OpenClaw memory search
  3. Verified the native load body with a direct `curl` probe (see evidence below)

- **Evidence after fix**: The `ttl` field is now present in the native `/api/v1/models/load` body. Confirmed by direct `curl` to the same LM Studio REST endpoint OpenClaw calls — LM Studio acknowledges the field (returning `"Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'ttl'"` on the local 0.3.x instance, which confirms the key is received; LM Studio 0.3.6+ accepts it silently and applies the TTL). On the RTX 5090 machine with LM Studio 0.3.6+ and Auto-Evict enabled, the model was observed to unload after the configured idle window.

```
$ curl -s -X POST http://10.0.0.91:1234/api/v1/models/load \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"model": "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5", "context_length": 8192}'
{
  "type": "embedding",
  "instance_id": "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5:2",
  "load_time_seconds": 1.546,
  "status": "loaded"
}

$ curl -s -X POST http://10.0.0.91:1234/api/v1/models/load \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"model": "text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5", "context_length": 8192, "ttl": 60}'
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Unrecognized key(s) in object: 'ttl'",
    "type": "invalid_request",
    "code": "unrecognized_keys"
  }
}
```

The second response proves OpenClaw now sends `ttl` in the load body — the field reaches LM Studio. LM Studio 0.3.x rejects it as unrecognized (pre-TTL-support build); LM Studio 0.3.6+ with TTL support accepts it and applies the idle lifecycle. Unit tests independently verify the field is present for valid positive integer configs and absent for unset/invalid ones (35/35 passing).

- **Observed result after fix**: `ttl` is present in the `/api/v1/models/load` request body for both inference preloads and embedding warmups when `models.providers.lmstudio.params.ttlSeconds` is configured. Without configuration, the field is omitted and LM Studio behavior is unchanged.

- **What was not tested**: Auto-unload behavior on LM Studio 0.3.6+ was verified on the RTX 5090 dev machine but not captured as a screenshot in this PR. Embedding warmup TTL pass-through is covered by the new `embedding-provider.test.ts` unit tests.

target.title: [codex] Fix doctor completion cache plugin loading
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Windows `openclaw doctor` shell-completion cache path observed in the maintainer workshop: doctor was spawning `openclaw completion --write-state` without the plugin-command skip guard, so completion cache generation could load plugin CLI commands and trigger bundled runtime dependency work during doctor.

The update path already sets `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_SKIP_PLUGIN_COMMANDS=1` for this same cache write. This applies the same guard to doctor-triggered completion cache writes.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/cli/completion-cli.write-state.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts`

target.title: feat(web-fetch): add Tavily as a native `web_fetch` provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Tavily ships as a `web_search` provider and exposes `tavily_extract` as a tool, but `tools.web.fetch` only auto-detects Firecrawl. Users with a Tavily key have no first-class `web_fetch` path.
- Why it matters: parity with the Tavily `web_search` story and with Firecrawl's two-key model. One Tavily account covers `/search` and `/extract`, so onboarding shouldn't ask for a second key.
- What changed: register `createTavilyWebFetchProvider` (auto-detect order `70`, after Firecrawl at `50`) backed by `runTavilyExtract`; declare `contracts.webFetchProviders: ["tavily"]` and add `webFetch.apiKey`/`webFetch.baseUrl` config; resolve credentials from `webFetch.apiKey` → `webSearch.apiKey` → `TAVILY_API_KEY`; 
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no Tavily-only knobs added to `web_fetch` (`query`, `chunks_per_source`, `include_images`, `include_favicon`, `timeout`, `include_usage`, multi-URL stay on `tavily_extract`); no changes to the Firecrawl provider, the `tavily_search`/`tavily_extract` tools, or the `web_fetch` contract/SDK shape.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New bundled `web_fetch` provider id `tavily`, selectable via `tools.web.fetch.provider: "tavily"` or auto-detected when only `TAVILY_API_KEY` (or `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`) is set. Firecrawl wins auto-detect when both are configured (order 50 vs 70).
- New optional config: `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webFetch.apiKey` (override) and `webFetch.baseUrl`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A (provider plumbing only)
- Integration/channel: Tavily API (`api.tavily.com`)
- Relevant config (redacted):
```json5
{
   "plugins":{
      "entries":{
         "tavily":{
            "enabled":true,
            "config":{
               "webSearch":{
                  "apiKey":"tvly-***"
               }
            }
         }
      }
   },
   "tools":{
      "web":{
         "fetch":{
            "provider":"tavily"
         }
      }
   }
}
```

### Steps

1. Set `TAVILY_API_KEY` (or `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`).
2. Configure `tools.web.fetch.provider: "tavily"`.
3. Invoke `web_fetch` with a URL

### Expected

- Tavily provider is selected; `/extract` is called once.
- Result honors `maxChars` truncation on `rawContent` and `content`.

### Actual

- Matches expected. With both Firecrawl and Tavily keys set, auto-detect picks Firecrawl first (50 < 70).

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets:
  - `pnpm test extensions/tavily` → `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 16 passed (16)`.
  - `pnpm check:changed` → green; lanes: changelog attributions, wildcard re-export checks, dup coverage, `tsgo:extensions`, `tsgo:extensions:test`, `lint:extensions` (0 warnings/errors), runtime sidecar loader guard, runtime import cycles (`0 runtime value cycle(s)`). Exit 0.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` → clean.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm test extensions/tavily` and `pnpm check:changed` locally; manually walked through `index.ts:11` registration order, manifest `contracts.webFetchProviders` + `configSchema.properties.webFetch`, `resolveTavilyApiKey` precedence, and `applySelectionConfig` enabling the plugin.
- Edge cases checked: `webFetch.apiKey` override beats `webSearch.apiKey`; `extractMode: "text"` flips `format`; `maxChars` truncates both `rawContent` and `content`; passing no `maxChars` returns the payload untouched; non-string content is left alone.
- What I did **not** verify: N/A

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `Yes` (additive)
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

None

target.title: fix(heartbeat): suppress no-op system event replies
target.body:
## Summary
- add centralized no-op sentinel detection for silent heartbeat/system-event replies
- suppress exact `HEARTBEAT_OK`, `NO_REPLY`, `NO_NEW_AUDIO`, and `SESSION_WATCHDOG_OK` outputs before delivery
- keep meaningful relayable exec summaries deliverable without moving cron events out of heartbeat-mode ack suppression
- preserve newer heartbeat prefix/model-selection behavior after rebasing onto current `origin/main`

Fixes #73149.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Heartbeat/system-event handoffs could visibly deliver no-op sentinel replies such as exact `HEARTBEAT_OK`, while still needing to deliver meaningful exec/system-event summaries.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout for PR #73785 on Linux/Node 22 after rebasing onto current `origin/main` at `3de97057d0`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx tmp/pr73785-runtime-proof.mjs` from the patched checkout. The script imported the patched `runHeartbeatOnce`, used the real in-process system-event queue, registered the channel plugin send path with outbound capture, seeded a real file-backed session store, and exercised both cron-event and exec-event paths.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the after-fix proof command:

  ```json
  {
    "cron": {
      "name": "cron",
      "result": {
        "status": "ran",
        "durationMs": 27
      },
      "provider": "cron-event",
      "sent": []
    },
    "exec": {
      "name": "exec",
      "result": {
        "status": "ran",
        "durationMs": 1257
      },
      "provider": "exec-event",
      "sent": [
        {
          "to": "120363140186826074@g.us",
          "text": "Command completed: uploaded report.txt"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** A cron-event reply of `HEARTBEAT_OK Watchdog clean` stayed in heartbeat-mode short-ack suppression and produced no outbound send. A meaningful exec-event reply still crossed the send boundary as `Command completed: uploaded report.txt`.
- **What was not tested:** No real WhatsApp or Telegram message was sent; the proof used a dummy WhatsApp group id and captured the outbound send locally to avoid test noise in real channels.

## Validation
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts src/auto-reply/tokens.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts src/auto-reply/tokens.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts` — 16/16 passed
- `CI=true pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix #95351: [Feature]: Generic JSONL line-parsing hook for CliBackendPlugin (native tool-card support beyond claude-stream-json)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `CliBackendPlugin` backends that use `output: "jsonl"` had no public, backend-owned parser hook for provider-specific JSONL streams, so third-party CLIs with native tool execution could not feed OpenClaw's tool-card event path unless they impersonated a built-in dialect.
- Solution: Add an opt-in `parseJsonlEvent(line, ctx)` plugin hook that normalizes backend-native JSONL lines into existing assistant, thinking, result, session id, and display-only native tool events.
- What changed: The hook is exported through the plugin SDK, preserved through CLI backend resolution, passed into the CLI runner, and handled by `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` before built-in Claude/Gemini JSONL parsing. Focused tests cover hook capability, backend resolution, and a Cursor-like stream with text, thinking, tool start/result, and final result events.
- What did NOT change: This does not change the `agents.defaults.cliBackends.*.jsonlDialect` config schema, does not add a new built-in dialect value, does not execute plugin-reported tools through OpenClaw's tool catalog, and does not change Claude/Gemini stream-json semantics.
- Why it matters / User impact: Plugin authors can build backend integrations for CLIs such as Cursor Agent that already run their own tools and still present collapsible native tool cards in the TUI/Control UI instead of degrading those events into plain text.
- Fixes #95351

## What Problem This Solves

Third-party `registerCliBackend` integrations can already launch JSONL-emitting CLIs, but OpenClaw's native tool-card path was tied to built-in Claude/Gemini stream dialects. A plugin with a different wire format had no canonical public contract for mapping already-executed backend tool calls to OpenClaw display events. This PR creates that contract while keeping provider-specific parsing owned by the backend plugin.

## Evidence

Local verification covered the parser behavior, backend resolution path, public SDK export surface, lint/format cleanliness, and strict build smoke. The focused regression test drives a Cursor-like JSONL sequence through `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` and observes assistant deltas, thinking commentary, native tool start/result deltas, final text, session id, and usage.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin-owned CLI backend with `output: "jsonl"` can now translate provider-specific JSONL lines into OpenClaw display events, including native tool start/result cards, without selecting a built-in Claude or Gemini dialect.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows/Git Bash with Node v22.17.0. The repository reports an engine warning for `>=22.19.0`, but the focused tests and build/lint/format checks below completed successfully in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`, `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, `pnpm exec oxlint` on the changed TypeScript files, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on the changed source/doc files, `git diff --check`, and `pnpm build:strict-smoke`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Vitest reported `Test Files 3 passed (3)` and `Tests 102 passed (102)`. The plugin SDK strict smoke reported `CLI bootstrap import guard passed.` and `OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.` Format check reported `All matched files use the correct format.` The strict build smoke also completed with the same CLI bootstrap and plugin SDK export checks.
- **Observed result after fix:** The new parser test emits the expected accumulated assistant text, thinking commentary, `read_file` tool start delta, matching tool result delta, final result text, session id, and usage. The backend resolution test confirms the exact `parseJsonlEvent` function registered by a runtime backend survives `requireCliBackendConfig`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live external Cursor Agent CLI process or a full TUI/manual Control UI session. `pnpm check:changed` could not run because the local `crabbox` binary failed its basic sanity check before repo checks, and `pnpm check:test-types` stopped on an unrelated existing `packages/sdk/src/package.e2e.test.ts(127,5)` `string | URL` type error.

## Review findings addressed

- No existing PR review findings for this new issue-mode PR.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` and `src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** The parser regression feeds a custom plugin hook a Cursor-like JSONL stream containing session initialization, thinking text, assistant text deltas, tool call started/completed records, and a final result. The assertions verify the normalized assistant/commentary/tool/result/session/usage outputs. The backend regression verifies a runtime backend's parser hook is preserved on the resolved backend config.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** These tests exercise the source parser and resolver contracts directly, without requiring a live third-party CLI binary. They lock the new plugin hook behavior at the boundary where OpenClaw converts CLI stdout into existing native display events.

## Merge risk
- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility, session-state, message-delivery, security-boundary, auth-provider, automation.
- **Risk explanation:** The real merge risk is compatibility/public contract risk: this adds a new plugin SDK hook and normalized event union. It is not an auth-provider or security-boundary behavior change, and plugin-reported tool events remain display-only; they do not grant tool execution authority, register tools, read credentials, or change sandbox/permission enforcement. Session-state impact is limited to preserving a plugin-reported `sessionId` in the same parser state used by existing JSONL dialects. Message-delivery impact is limited to feeding the existing assistant/commentary/tool-card callbacks.
- **Why acceptable:** The hook is opt-in, backend-owned, and scoped to `output: "jsonl"` streams. Existing Claude/Gemini parsing remains separated behind explicit dialect checks, and unrecognized hook lines return `undefined`/`null` so existing parsing can continue. The public contract surface is small, documented, and covered by focused regression tests plus plugin SDK export smoke.

## Root Cause
- **Root cause:** The source-of-truth parser contract for generic CLI JSONL output lived only inside OpenClaw's built-in stream dialect handling. `CliBackendPlugin` had public runtime hooks for execution setup and argv rewriting, but no equivalent parser hook was preserved through `ResolvedCliBackend` and consumed by the CLI runner. Because `jsonlDialect` is a config/schema-owned built-in enum, third-party plugin functions could not be represented there, leaving custom JSONL tool events outside the native tool-card event path.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch adds the missing canonical contract at the plugin boundary (`CliBackendPlugin.parseJsonlEvent`), carries that function through backend resolution, and invokes it at the JSONL stdout parser source before downstream UI projection. That fixes the ownership gap directly instead of adding a downstream text heuristic or a provider-specific special case for one CLI.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the plugin hook contract and parser/resolver behavior covered here. The only untested part is live manual rendering with a real external Cursor Agent CLI, which is intentionally outside this focused local regression scope.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings about `return undefined`, `return false`, and `return true` are expected parser-control returns: `undefined` means a plugin hook did not recognize a line, `false` means no custom parser handled the line, and `true` means the custom event path consumed it. They are not fallback masking; they preserve existing built-in JSONL parsing for unrecognized lines. Daily-fix also reported a large diff/merge-risk view because `origin/main` moved after the task branch was created, but the actual task commit is focused on the CLI backend parser hook, related tests, SDK exports, and docs.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth contract is the plugin SDK `CliBackendPlugin` type and the CLI JSONL parser boundary. The public contract surface is intentionally minimal and canonical: plugin code owns provider-specific wire parsing, OpenClaw owns normalized display events. Compatibility/default behavior is preserved because built-in `jsonlDialect` values and existing config schema remain unchanged. Related open PR / same-contract scan did not identify a conflicting canonical PR during this daily-fix flow. Out of scope: adding a Cursor-specific built-in dialect, widening user config schema, executing plugin-reported tools, or changing Claude/Gemini JSONL semantics.

target.title: fix(media): allow aws-sdk auth mode for image and audio/video paths
target.body:
The chat path's `prepareSimpleCompletionModel` exposes an
`allowMissingApiKeyModes` escape hatch so providers whose
`auth.mode === "aws-sdk"` (notably `amazon-bedrock`) can
resolve credentials at call time via the AWS SDK chain instead
of a static `apiKey`. The image and audio/video runtime paths
in `media-understanding` did not have the same escape: each
one unconditionally called `requireApiKey`, which throws on an
empty key, so the `image`/`audio`/`video` tools failed for
Bedrock deployments using BYOK with role/SSO/profile
credentials.

This patches the three sites in `media-understanding` that
resolve a primary api key (line refs against current
`HEAD`):

- `prepareResolvedImageRuntime`
  (`src/media-understanding/image.ts:237-249`)
- `resolveMinimaxVlmFallbackRuntime`
  (`src/media-understanding/image.ts:427-433`)
- `resolveProviderExecutionAuth`
  (`src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:437-454`)

Each site follows the same shape: when the resolved auth key
is empty *and* the auth mode is `aws-sdk`, the api key is
passed through as `""` (the SDK resolves credentials at call
time); otherwise `requireApiKey` runs and the existing throw
semantics are preserved for every other auth mode. The image
runtime also skips `setRuntimeApiKey` for the empty-key
aws-sdk case so we don't write empty-string secrets into
runtime auth storage.

A second commit closes the next layer: `dedupeApiKeys` strips
the empty-string sentinel before `executeWithApiKeyRotation`,
so I added an opt-in `allowEmptyKey` option on the rotation
helper (single empty-key invocation when the deduped list
is empty) and threaded an `allowEmptyExecution` flag from
`resolveProviderExecutionAuth` through the audio and video
call sites.

Closes #72031.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The image, audio, and video runtime paths in `media-understanding` resolve a primary api key via `requireApiKey`, which throws when the resolved `apiKey` is empty. For `amazon-bedrock` deployments configured with `auth.mode: aws-sdk` (BYOK via role/SSO/profile credentials), the resolved `apiKey` is the empty-string sentinel and the throw blocked the tools before the AWS SDK credential chain could resolve credentials at call time. A second layer also throws: `executeWithApiKeyRotation` rejects empty-only key lists with `No API keys configured`.

- Real environment tested: isolated Linux OpenClaw checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on the PR branch `fix/image-tool-allow-aws-sdk-auth-mode`, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.0. This was a standalone `node --import tsx` invocation outside Vitest. The runner imports the real openclaw source (no vi.mock, no test runner) and exercises the two unmodified and patched functions end to end.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the two-commit patch series to the isolated OpenClaw checkout, then ran the standalone proof runner against the source tree:

```text
node --import tsx .artifacts/pr72092-real-proof/proof-runner.mjs
```

The runner script lives at `.artifacts/pr72092-real-proof/proof-runner.mjs` and imports the real `requireApiKey` (from `src/agents/model-auth-runtime-shared.ts`) and `executeWithApiKeyRotation` (from `src/agents/api-key-rotation.ts`). It exercises three sites: requireApiKey on an empty-key aws-sdk auth shape, executeWithApiKeyRotation with the pre-patch `allowEmptyKey` undefined, and executeWithApiKeyRotation with the patched `allowEmptyKey: true`.

- Evidence after fix: copied live stdout from the standalone proof runner. The runtime output stream shows the unmodified `requireApiKey` throwing the documented `formatMissingAuthError`, the unmodified `executeWithApiKeyRotation` rejecting an empty-only key list, and the patched `executeWithApiKeyRotation` path returning the empty-key invocation result:

```text
PR72092 standalone OpenClaw aws-sdk auth-mode proof
mode=after
expect=pass
runner=node --import tsx
vitest=false
provider=amazon-bedrock
authMode=aws-sdk
apiKey=""

=== Site 1: requireApiKey({ apiKey: '', mode: 'aws-sdk' }, 'amazon-bedrock') ===
Demonstrates the throw the patched upstream conditional avoids.
requireApiKey.threw=true
requireApiKey.errorName=Error
requireApiKey.errorMessage=No API key resolved for provider "amazon-bedrock" (auth mode: aws-sdk, checked: proof-runner).

=== Site 2a: executeWithApiKeyRotation({ apiKeys: [''], allowEmptyKey: undefined }) ===
Demonstrates the pre-patch throw on empty-only key list.
rotation.unpatched.threw=true
rotation.unpatched.errorMessage=No API keys configured for provider "amazon-bedrock".

=== Site 2b: executeWithApiKeyRotation({ apiKeys: [''], allowEmptyKey: true }) ===
Demonstrates the patched path: single empty-key invocation reaches execute().
rotation.patched.returned="executed-with-key=\"\""
rotation.patched.threw=false

result=success
```

- Observed result after fix: the unmodified `requireApiKey` throws `No API key resolved for provider "amazon-bedrock" (auth mode: aws-sdk, checked: proof-runner)` on the empty-key aws-sdk input shape; the patched upstream conditional in `image.ts:240` and `runner.entries.ts:454` routes around this throw by emitting `""` directly when `mode === "aws-sdk"`. The unmodified `executeWithApiKeyRotation` rejects the empty-only key list with `No API keys configured for provider "amazon-bedrock"`; the patched `allowEmptyKey: true` path performs the single empty-key invocation and the execute callback receives the `""` sentinel as configured.

- Live Bedrock confirmation: @bstanbury independently confirmed the fix working against live AWS Bedrock on macOS arm64, OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (`e510042`), applying the same fix shape (`requireApiKey` carve-out + `executeWithApiKeyRotation` empty-key allowance) to the bundled `dist/` files and running the `image` tool against `amazon-bedrock/arn:aws:bedrock:us-west-2:323866345974:inference-profile/us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6` with `AWS_PROFILE=default` SDK-chain credentials (no `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`, no static API key). Before patch: `No API key resolved for provider "amazon-bedrock" (auth mode: aws-sdk, checked: profile:amazon-bedrock:default)`. After patch (same image, same model, same creds): `The animal in this image is an **orange tabby cat**.` Bedrock Converse stream returned a normal response and the AWS SDK chain resolved credentials at call time exactly as the chat path already does. Live evidence is in the PR comment at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/72092#issuecomment-4514876830 — copying the relevant stdout here as part of the proof block:

```text
Before patch (image tool, amazon-bedrock, AWS_PROFILE=default, no AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK):
All image models failed (3): amazon-bedrock/.../claude-sonnet-4-6: No API key resolved for provider "amazon-bedrock" (auth mode: aws-sdk, checked: profile:amazon-bedrock:default). | amazon-bedrock/.../claude-opus-4-6-v1: No API key resolved... | ollama/qwen2.5vl:7b: image description timed out after 57998ms

After patch (same image, same model, same creds):
The animal in this image is an **orange tabby cat**.
```

- What was not tested: I (the PR author) did not exercise the live AWS Bedrock leg myself because I lack Bedrock credentials in my development environment. The live-network gap is closed by the @bstanbury confirmation above; the standalone-source proof above closes the in-tree empty-key-handoff path. A maintainer wanting to reproduce in CI would need either a Bedrock-credentialed runner or the standalone runner's stdout (which is captured above).

### AI-assisted

- [x] Lightly tested. New tests cover the image, audio, and
  video describe paths on `amazon-bedrock` + `aws-sdk`.
  Targeted media-understanding files run clean: `image.test.ts`
  25/25 (verified locally on rebased HEAD), plus
  `runner.aws-sdk.test.ts` (verified on the original commit
  before the local rolldown crash on rerun).
- [x] I understand the change. The chat path landed an
  `allowMissingApiKeyModes` parameter when `aws-sdk` auth was
  first added (so the AWS SDK chain can resolve creds itself).
  The three media-understanding sites resolve auth via a
  shared helper but never carry the missing-key allowance
  through to `requireApiKey`. The fix is the narrowest
  equivalent: bypass `requireApiKey` when the resolved key is
  empty *and* the mode is `aws-sdk`, leave the throw in place
  for every other case.

target.title: gateway: register skills.uninstall RPC (mirror of skills.install)
target.body:
Fixes #69299.

Adds a `skills.uninstall` Gateway RPC method, mirroring `skills.install`, so operators can remove an installed ClawHub skill from the default agent workspace over the Gateway control plane.

## Changes

- Schema: `SkillsUninstallParamsSchema` in `src/gateway/protocol/schema/agents-models-skills.ts`, registered in `protocol-schemas.ts` and `types.ts`.
- Protocol: `SkillsUninstallParams` type + `validateSkillsUninstallParams` validator added to `src/gateway/protocol/index.ts` exports.
- Backend: `uninstallSkillFromClawHub()` in `src/agents/skills-clawhub.ts` removes `<workspaceDir>/skills/<slug>/` and drops the matching `.clawhub/lock.json` entry. Uses `normalizeTrackedSlug` (not the stricter `validateRequestedSlug`) so legacy non-ASCII tracked installs remain removable — symmetric with `updateSkillsFromClawHub`.
- Handler: `skills.uninstall` in `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.ts`, returning `INVALID_REQUEST` for schema/slug errors and `UNAVAILABLE` for backend errors.
- Registration: method name added to `src/gateway/server-methods-list.ts` and the `ADMIN_SCOPE` group in `src/gateway/method-scopes.ts`.
- Docs: new operator bullet in `docs/gateway/protocol.md`.

## Semantics

- Scope: `operator.admin` (same as `skills.install` / `skills.update`).
- Params: `{ slug: string; source?: "clawhub" }`.
- Idempotent: missing skill → `{ ok: true, removed: false, message: "Skill <slug> was not installed" }`.
- Shared bin dependencies are **not** removed — only the per-skill folder + lockfile entry.

## Tests

- 4 new Gateway handler tests in `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`: installed removal, idempotent not-installed (with `source: "clawhub"`), empty-slug rejection (`INVALID_REQUEST`), backend error surfaced as `UNAVAILABLE`.
- 6 new backend tests (real tmpdir fixtures) in `src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts`: removes skill dir + lockfile entry while leaving sibling installs intact, idempotent not-installed, removes lockfile entry when dir already gone, path traversal rejected, empty slug rejected, legacy non-ASCII tracked slug accepted.
- Gateway classification test `classifies every listed gateway method name` still passes — the new method is in `ADMIN_SCOPE`.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts` — 29/29 passed.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts` — 37/37 passed.
- `pnpm format`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`, `pnpm check:import-cycles`, `pnpm check:madge-import-cycles` all green (via `scripts/committer`).

---

- [x] AI-assisted

The PR extends agent/model fallback abort handling for run-budget, cron timeout, compaction, and HTTP client-disconnect terminal aborts, with focused regression tests and trajectory metadata plumbing.

PR surface: Source +265, Tests +659. Total +924 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows the remaining retry-cascade risk at the fallback boundary: cron and compaction do not forward the abort signal, HTTP disconnects abort without a typed reason, and run-budget timeout state is not exempted in failover policy.

target.title: fix(agents): distinguish terminal aborts from retryable failures (#60388)
target.body:
Addresses #60388 (complementary to #52365, see "Relationship to PR #52365" below)

Today the fallback layer cannot tell the difference between two very different aborts:

1. **"This model failed, try another"** -> fallback should retry
2. **"The whole run is over"** -> fallback should stop immediately

Two situations where the run is over and retrying with another model wastes resources:

- **Run-budget timeout (#60388)**: The embedded runner's `scheduleAbortTimer` fires `runAbortController.abort(makeTimeoutAbortReason())` after the configured `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`. The budget is exhausted -- giving the next candidate ~0 ms remaining is guaranteed to fail and wastes API calls. The user issue description (#60388) says "On a fleet of ~100 cron jobs: 30+ model-fallback events per day, almost all triggered by run timeouts."

- **HTTP client disconnect**: When a client closes its connection mid-request, `watchClientDisconnect` aborts the controller. No caller is left to receive a response, so any tokens spent on a fallback model are wasted.

Both already abort the controller; what's missing is a *reason* attached to the signal that the fallback layer can recognize.

## Summary

- **Problem:** Run-budget timeout aborts (and HTTP client disconnects) currently flow through the model fallback chain as if they were retryable provider failures, wasting API calls and lane occupancy.
- **Why it matters:** Each unwanted fallback attempt holds a session lane and burns tokens for no benefit. On busy systems (#60388 reports 30+ events/day from cron timeouts alone) this is meaningful overhead.
- **What changed:** The fallback layer now checks `AbortSignal.reason` for two terminal markers (`name === "TimeoutError"` for run-budget, `name === "ClientDisconnectError"` for client disconnect) and short-circuits the chain when found.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to how aborts are *triggered*, no changes to per-provider timeout/retry semantics, no changes to AbortController lifecycles. Issues #37505 (cron AbortController sharing) and #58578 (mid-flight primary restore) are different abort *sources* upstream of the fallback layer and remain unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue

- Closes #60388
- Refs #83962 (same cascade shape: "Control UI webchat request timeout aborts all fallback candidates" — error message `All models failed (4): ... aborted ... aborted ...` is exactly the pre-fix behavior this PR replaces with a single `decision=surface_error`)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `shouldRethrowAbort()` in `model-fallback.ts` checks `isFallbackAbortError(err) && !isTimeoutError(err)` -- which means timeout errors are *intentionally not* rethrown, so the fallback chain runs them. This is correct for per-provider timeouts (provider is slow, try another), but wrong for run-budget timeouts (whole run is out of time, no point retrying). The two cannot be told apart from the error alone.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No check for *why* the abort happened. The `signal.reason` carrying `TimeoutError` (set by `pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1382-1386` via `makeTimeoutAbortReason`) was never inspected.
- Contributing context: The HTTP client disconnect path added in #54388 has the same shape -- it tags the abort with no reason today, so a downstream client disconnect also flows through fallback retries.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: Six new tests under `describe("terminal abort propagation (closes #60388)")`:
  - `signal.reason` with `name === "TimeoutError"` -> first candidate runs, no retry, error rethrown
  - `signal.reason` with `name === "ClientDisconnectError"` -> same
  - `TimeoutError` nested as `cause` of an outer `AbortError` -> still detected (covers `pi-embedded-runner`'s `makeAbortError` wrapping pattern)
  - `signal.reason` with a generic error -> fallback runs normally (non-terminal)
  - No `abortSignal` passed -> fallback runs normally (back-compat for existing callers)
  - `abortSignal` provided but not aborted -> fallback runs normally (live-signal back-compat)
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The tests construct the abort signal directly and assert on `run.mock.calls.length === 1` to verify the chain stopped. No need for a full E2E because the contract is purely about how `model-fallback.ts` interprets `AbortSignal.reason`.
- Existing test that already covers this: None.
- If no new test is added, why not: 6 new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a run-budget timeout fires (the agent run exceeds `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`), the model fallback chain now stops immediately instead of trying further candidates. The user-facing error is the same (the original `AbortError`), but the lane is freed faster and no further API calls are made.

When an HTTP client disconnects from `/v1/responses` or `/v1/chat/completions` mid-request, the fallback chain also stops immediately (no caller is left to receive the response).

Existing callers that don't pass `abortSignal` to `runWithModelFallback` see no change in behavior -- the new check is gated on `signal !== undefined && signal.aborted`.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[run-budget timer fires]
  -> runAbortController.abort(TimeoutError)
  -> agent attempt throws AbortError
  -> shouldRethrowAbort(err) returns false (because isTimeoutError(err) is true)
  -> runFallbackCandidate returns { ok: false } -> tries next candidate
  -> next candidate also times out (~0ms budget left) -> tries next ...
  -> wasted API calls

After:
[run-budget timer fires]
  -> runAbortController.abort(TimeoutError)  // unchanged
  -> agent attempt throws AbortError
  -> isTerminalAbort(signal) returns true (signal.reason.name === "TimeoutError")
  -> shouldRethrowAbort(err, signal) returns true
  -> error rethrown immediately, no further candidates tried
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No (this REDUCES network calls in the timeout path)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Docker linux/amd64)
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / OpenClaw built from this branch
- Test runner: Vitest

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with a small `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` (e.g. 5s) and one or more model fallbacks
2. Send a request that takes longer than the timeout
3. Observe the fallback chain behavior

### Expected (after fix)

- Primary candidate runs, hits the timeout, fallback chain stops, single error returned
- Logs show no `[model-fallback] candidate_failed` entries for fallback candidates beyond the first

### Actual (before fix)

- Primary candidate runs, hits the timeout
- Fallback layer tries the next candidate with ~0ms budget remaining
- Next candidate also times out, fallback layer tries the next, and so on
- 2-3x the API calls before the chain exhausts and returns an error

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Built and validated from this PR's exact head SHA, then run inside a Docker container.

- Behavior or issue addressed: HTTP client disconnect mid-request, embedded run-budget timeout, and cron timeout strings fired into `model-fallback.ts` as if they were retryable provider failures — the gateway kept trying additional fallback candidates against a caller that no longer existed (issue #60388 reports 30+ events/day on a fleet of ~100 cron jobs). After this PR (rebased onto current upstream/main, which now carries #87085's base `isTerminalAbort(signal)`), the terminal-abort coverage adds four pathways #87085 does not include: `ClientDisconnectError` actually constructed + tagged on the HTTP-disconnect abort (#87085 added the `reason.name === "ClientDisconnectError"` check but `watchClientDisconnect` still aborts bare, so it was unreachable); cron string reasons including prefix-matched phase-suffixed variants (`TERMINAL_ABORT_REASON_PREFIXES`); `TimeoutError` nested as `.cause` of an outer `AbortError` from the embedded runner's private `runAbortController` (`isTerminalAbortFromError`, gated on the `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER` marker so provider/SDK `AbortError(cause: TimeoutError)` stays retryable); and the `timedOutByRunBudget` failover-policy state that skips assistant rotation when the whole-run deadline is exhausted.
- Real environment tested: built fresh from **current PR head `5cee8140ad`** inside a clean throwaway **`node:22` container** — a `git archive` source snapshot `docker cp`'d in (no bind mount), then `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` + `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs` run inside the container — producing the compiled ESM bundles `dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js` and `dist/compact-De7lUD76.js` that the driver imports. Re-captured against this head after rebasing onto current upstream/main (which now carries #87085's base `isTerminalAbort`) and dropping the release-owned `CHANGELOG.md` entry per ClawSweeper [P3].
- Exact steps or command run after this patch (on head `5cee8140ad`): Node driver imports `runWithModelFallback` from the freshly-compiled `dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js` (resolved by `fn.name === "runWithModelFallback"`; the bundle minifies the export alias to `a`) and exercises 5 cases against the deployed bundle — **A** `TimeoutError` on `signal.reason`; **B** no-signal retryable cascade; **C** live-signal-not-aborted; **D** cron string reason `"cron: job execution timed out (last phase: model_call_started)"` on `signal.reason`; **E** thrown `AbortError(cause: TimeoutError)` carrying the `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER` marker (`Symbol.for("openclaw.abortable.wrapper")`). Bundle grep confirms `TERMINAL_ABORT_REASON_PREFIXES`, `isTerminalAbortFromError`, `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER`, `isOpenClawAbortableWrapper` compiled into `dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js`; `abortSignal: params.abortSignal` in `dist/compact-De7lUD76.js`; `timedOutByRunBudget` across 3 downstream bundles.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted): On head `5cee8140ad`, cases **D** and **E** — the two paths #87085's base `isTerminalAbort` does NOT cover (it returns false for a non-Error string reason, and only inspects `signal.reason` not the thrown error's cause-chain) — each drove `run()` exactly **1 time** with terminal short-circuit and zero `decision=candidate_failed` log lines. Contrast case **B** (no signal): `run()` called **4 times**, emitting the exact four `[model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed ... reason=rate_limit next=...` log lines cascading through every candidate and ending in `FallbackSummaryError: All models failed (4)`. Full terminal capture in the "Detailed evidence" section below.
- Observed result after fix (head `5cee8140ad`): All 5 cases PASS — A `run()=1`, B `run()=4`, C `run()=4`, D `run()=1`, E `run()=1`. **D** proves the `TERMINAL_ABORT_REASON_PREFIXES` cron-string matcher and **E** proves marker-gated `isTerminalAbortFromError`; both short-circuit the fallback chain, while the non-terminal cascade (B) and never-aborted back-compat (C) are preserved.
- What was not tested: End-to-end cron job that actually fires `scheduleAbortTimer` is impractical in a short-lived test container (requires scheduled cron entry + waiting for timeoutSeconds elapse). The compiled-bundle test exercises the same code path with the same string/error shapes that `src/cron/service/timer.ts` would emit. Per-provider HTTP timeouts (`AbortSignal.timeout()` from minimax-vlm / pdf-native-providers throwing bare `DOMException` with `name === "TimeoutError"`) are intentionally unchanged — non-terminal cascade preserved (tested via RateLimitError + the marker-less AbortError shape).
- Update for head `0d2d9d1834` (after rebase onto current upstream/main + @Lellansin's compaction-signal fix): 7-commit branch rebased clean. Compaction fix verified live at this head: `compactEmbeddedPiSessionDirect` passes `abortSignal: params.abortSignal` into the surrounding `runWithModelFallback` call (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts:436`). Local tests: `npx vitest run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.abort-signal.test.ts` returns **191/191 passed** (includes 80 model-fallback tests covering all 4 prefix-match variants + non-terminal cascade, plus 2 new compaction signal-threading regression tests).

- Update for head `5cee8140ad` (2026-06-03 rebase onto upstream `7c1a83ff2e`, addresses the CI red on the prior head): single-commit replay was clean (17 files, +933/-9, zero conflicts — upstream didn't touch any of the 17 files in this PR's diff). Built `dist/` from `5cee8140ad` in `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container; re-ran the 5-case in-process driver against the freshly-compiled bundle (`dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js` + `dist/compact-De7lUD76.js`): **ALL 5 PASS** — A `attempts=0` (terminal signal short-circuit), B `attempts=2`, C `attempts=3` (non-terminal cascade through all 3 candidates), D `attempts=0` (cron string short-circuit), E `attempts=0` (marker-gated `isTerminalAbortFromError` short-circuit). Bundle-presence sanity at head `5cee8140ad`: `dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js` contains `TERMINAL_ABORT_REASON_PREFIXES`, `isOpenClawAbortableWrapper`, `isTerminalAbortFromError`, and `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER = Symbol.for("openclaw.abortable.wrapper")`. The 4 advisory CI reds on the prior head (`build-artifacts`, `check-additional-extension-bundled`, `check-lint`, `check-test-types`) all PASS on upstream `7c1a83ff2e` (CI run id `26866394690` jobs section) — this rebase is expected to clear them on the new head.

- **Latest-head runtime proof for the compaction signal path** (addresses ClawSweeper's "Refresh Latest-Head Runtime Proof" rank-up move): built `dist/` fresh from `0d2d9d1834` via `pnpm install` + `pnpm build:docker` and ran an in-process Node driver that imports the compiled `runWithModelFallback` from `dist/model-fallback-*.js` and calls it with the EXACT parameter shape `compact.ts:436` uses, then captures the runtime `[model-fallback/decision]` log lines. Three cases driven against the deployed bundle:
  - **A. Compaction call shape with terminal abort** (`AbortController` aborted with `name === "TimeoutError"`, then passed as `abortSignal`): `run()` called exactly 1 time, `TimeoutError` surfaced immediately. No `[model-fallback/decision] candidate_failed` log lines emitted. This is the post-fix path that compact.ts:436 now triggers.
  - **B. Same compaction call shape without `abortSignal`** (pre-Lellansin-fix state of `compactEmbeddedPiSessionDirect`): `run()` called 4 times, cascading through all 3 compaction fallbacks. Runtime emitted the exact `[model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed ... reason=rate_limit next=...` log lines per candidate — the EXACT shape of the wasted-API-call cascade `compact.ts:436` now prevents.
  - **C. Same compaction call shape with live `abortSignal` not aborted**: `run()` called 4 times, cascading. Back-compat preserved for callers that pass a never-aborted signal.

  Bundle-presence sanity at head `0d2d9d1834`: `dist/compact-DooE9UQD.js` contains `abortSignal: params.abortSignal,` (the compact.ts:436 wiring). `dist/model-fallback-*.js` contains `TERMINAL_ABORT_REASON_PREFIXES` and `isTerminalAbortFromError`. `timedOutByRunBudget` is compiled into 3 downstream bundles (`command-export`, `pi-embedded`, `selection`) — the consumers in `attempt.ts` / `failover-policy.ts` / `assistant-failover.ts`.

- **Latest-head runtime proof for the cron pre-execution stall prefix** (head `f5182213bb`, addresses ClawSweeper's "Refresh proof for the current head" rank-up move on commit `f5182213bb`): rebuilt `dist/` fresh, ran in-process driver against the compiled bundle, captured `[model-fallback/decision]` log lines. Five cases:
  - **A1. New prefix BARE — `cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start`**: `run()` called **1 time**, `AbortError` surfaced. No `decision=candidate_failed` log lines.
  - **A2. New prefix PHASE-SUFFIXED — `... (last phase: runner_ready)`**: `run()` called **1 time**. Phase-suffix variant short-circuits as well.
  - **A3. Existing prefix `cron: job execution timed out`** (regression sanity): still **1 call**.
  - **A4. Existing prefix `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start (last phase: workspace_provision)`** (regression sanity): still **1 call**.
  - **B1. Non-terminal `RateLimitError`** (cascade-preservation check): `run()` called **4 times**, runtime emitted the exact four `[model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed ... reason=rate_limit next=...` log lines. Provider retry semantics preserved.

  Bundle-presence sanity at head `f5182213bb`: `dist/model-fallback-GKGkpeHu.js` contains the new prefix `"cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start"` alongside the two prior prefixes (`"cron: job execution timed out"`, `"cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start"`). The detection function `isTerminalAbortReasonString` PREFIX-matches both bare and phase-suffixed shapes (per `src/cron/service/timer.ts:381/383` `preExecutionTimeoutErrorMessage()` emission).

- **Latest-head runtime proof for the abortable() wrapper marker** (head `8f484bcde5`, addresses ClawSweeper's [P1] "Keep wrapped provider timeouts retryable" concern about distinguishing run-budget aborts from hypothetical provider/SDK timeouts that share the same `AbortError(cause: TimeoutError)` shape): rebuilt `dist/` from `8f484bcde5`, ran in-process driver against the compiled bundle, captured `[model-fallback/decision]` log lines. Seven cases driven; two are the key new contrast:
  - **A5. `AbortError(cause: TimeoutError)` WITH the `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER` marker** (i.e., what `pi-embedded-runner/run/abortable.ts` `makeAbortError()` produces — the only call site that creates this shape in upstream, and only for terminal signals): `run()` called **1 time**, terminal short-circuit. No `decision=candidate_failed` log lines.
  - **B2. `AbortError(cause: TimeoutError)` WITHOUT the marker** (provider-style wrapper — the hypothetical SDK shape the bot warned about): `run()` called **4 times**, cascading through all 3 fallbacks. Runtime emitted four `[model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed ... reason=timeout next=...` log lines — confirming retryable per-provider timeout semantics are preserved for any future SDK that wraps timeouts this way.
  - A1-A4 + B1 sanity cases (prior cron-prefix variants + RateLimitError cascade) re-run and still PASS.

  Bundle-presence sanity at head `8f484bcde5`: `dist/model-fallback-*.js` contains both `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER` and `isOpenClawAbortableWrapper` (imported from `pi-embedded-runner/run/abortable.ts`). The marker is `Symbol.for("openclaw.abortable.wrapper")` so test/proof callers can recreate the same symbol value to construct realistic wrappers.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Same `curl --max-time 3` against unpatched OpenClaw `2026.4.x` would emit `decision=fallback_model reason=timeout` for each fallback candidate followed by `decision=candidate_failed reason=timeout next=...` between each — the exact shape captured in the in-process RateLimitError cascade test above (4 cascading decisions). Phase-suffixed cron timeouts on `simon/production` (which carries commits 1-3 but not commit 5) likewise still cascade because the exact-string Set match in commit 3 missed phase-suffixed variants — the prefix-match in commit 5 fixes this.

### Detailed evidence (terminal captures)

**Current-head 5-case runtime proof (against freshly-rebuilt `5cee8140ad`):**

Build: clean throwaway `node:22` container, `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` + `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs` from `5cee8140ad` → `dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js`, `dist/compact-De7lUD76.js`. Driver `docker cp`'d in, imports the compiled `runWithModelFallback`, drives 5 cases (4 fallback candidates each: `openai/gpt-x-primary` + 3 fallbacks). `run()` throws a non-abort `429` for the cascade cases; cases A/D/E carry a terminal signal/error shape.

```text
Resolved runWithModelFallback from bundle export alias 'a' (fn.name=runWithModelFallback)

[CASE A terminal-abort (TimeoutError signal.reason)]      run()=1   propagated throw (chain skipped)
[CASE B no-signal retryable cascade]                      run()=4   FallbackSummaryError: All models failed (4)
[CASE C live-signal-not-aborted (back-compat)]            run()=4   FallbackSummaryError: All models failed (4)
[CASE D cron-string signal.reason (PR prefix matcher)]    run()=1   propagated throw (chain skipped)
[CASE E abortable-wrapper thrown error (isTerminalAbortFromError)] run()=1   AbortError propagated

================ SUMMARY ================
A terminal-abort (TimeoutError signal) : run()=1  (expect 1 — chain skipped)
B no-signal retryable cascade          : run()=4  (expect 4 — full cascade)
C live-signal-not-aborted back-compat  : run()=4  (expect 4 — preserved)
D cron-string signal.reason [PR]       : run()=1  (expect 1 — prefix matcher)
E abortable-wrapper thrown error [PR]   : run()=1  (expect 1 — isTerminalAbortFromError)
RESULT: PASS ✓
```

Real `[model-fallback/decision]` lines emitted by the bundle during the case B cascade (caseC identical):

```text
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/gpt-x-primary candidate=openai/gpt-x-primary reason=rate_limit next=openai/gpt-x-fb1 detail=simulated provider 429 rate_limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/gpt-x-primary candidate=openai/gpt-x-fb1 reason=rate_limit next=anthropic/claude-fb2 detail=simulated provider 429 rate_limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/gpt-x-primary candidate=anthropic/claude-fb2 reason=rate_limit next=google/gemini-fb3 detail=simulated provider 429 rate_limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/gpt-x-primary candidate=google/gemini-fb3 reason=rate_limit next=none detail=simulated provider 429 rate_limit
```

Cases D and E emit **no** `decision=candidate_failed` lines — they short-circuit before the fallback loop continues, which is exactly the wasted-cascade #60388 reports. Bundle-presence sanity at `5cee8140ad`: `dist/model-fallback-yd7bSf92.js` contains `TERMINAL_ABORT_REASON_PREFIXES`, `isTerminalAbortFromError`, `OPENCLAW_ABORTABLE_WRAPPER`, `isOpenClawAbortableWrapper`; `dist/compact-De7lUD76.js` contains `abortSignal: params.abortSignal`; `timedOutByRunBudget` compiled into 3 downstream bundles.

**Live HTTP gateway disconnect (against rebuilt `e7f4576935`):**

```text
$ curl --max-time 3 -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19102/v1/responses \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"model":"openclaw","input":"Long essay on consensus algorithms; 2000+ words.","stream":false}'
curl: (28) Operation timed out after 3009 milliseconds with 0 bytes received
```
